title: Blotterbook — completed backlog archive (Done items)
intro: Archived Done items moved out of backlog.json to keep the active backlog lean. Each retains its doneNote — the shipped record of what was actually done (prompts were already stripped per donePolicy). Newest work tends toward the end, mirroring the order items were completed in the live file.
note: Append-only archive of completed backlog items. When an item in backlog.json is set to `done`, move its object here. Admin-only and fetched no-store; like backlog.json it is EXCLUDED from the cache-busting manifest (build-manifest.mjs) and from version-bump path classification (bump-version.mjs), so editing it never churns the manifest or bumps the version. The admin Backlog panel fetches this file alongside backlog.json and merges the item lists.

items.title: Close the CSV→HTML injection (XSS)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `rootSym()` (`app/adapters.js:52`) now strips to `[A-Z0-9._-]` at the source, and `esc()` was applied at every sink (`app/app.js` cost table ~493, exported report ~1165, data-manager rows ~1385/1388, trade editor ~1421 — incl. `side`). Verified in-browser: `rootSym('MES')` → `MESIMGSRCXONERRORALERT1`; a stored payload renders as escaped text (no injected element, XSS never fired); legit symbols (MES/MNQ/MCL) still render; 55/55 adapter tests pass.

items.title: Add a Pages `_headers` file with CSP + security headers
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** root `_headers` file applies to `/*`: CSP (`default-src 'self'`; `img-src 'self' data:` for screenshots; `connect-src 'self'`; `object-src 'none'`; `base-uri 'self'`; `form-action 'self'`; `frame-ancestors 'none'`; no `'unsafe-eval'`), plus `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`, `Permissions-Policy` (camera/mic/geo/ topics off). `script-src`/`style-src` keep `'unsafe-inline'` (3 first-party inline scripts on home/ admin/changelog + inline `style=""` attrs; no build step for nonces yet — tighten via A1/A2). NOTE: `_headers` only takes effect on Cloudflare Pages, not the local static preview — confirm the response headers on the next deploy. App/demo/staging/home/admin all still render.

items.title: Replace the raw-ADMIN_KEY-to-browser flow with a short-lived signed token
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** new `functions/_lib/auth.js` issues/verifies HMAC-SHA256 tokens. `/api/admin-key` now returns a short-lived signed token (TTL `ADMIN_TOKEN_TTL_SEC`, default 2h; signed with `TOKEN_SECRET`→ falls back to `ADMIN_KEY`) instead of the raw key — the raw `ADMIN_KEY` never reaches the browser. The admin page stores/sends the token (x-admin-key header for writes; `bb_staging` cookie for staging launch). `/api/status`, `/api/config`, and the `_middleware` staging gate verify it via `isAdminAuthorized()`; the raw key stays accepted server-side as a fallback (never handed to a browser). Tokens expire. Verified with a Node WebCrypto test (valid/tamper/expiry/fallback). Full E2E needs the deployed Access+KV env.

items.title: Verify the Cloudflare Access JWT signature (defense-in-depth)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `/api/admin-key` verifies the `Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion` RS256 signature against the team JWKS (`<ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN>/cdn-cgi/access/certs`, cached 1h) plus audience (`ACCESS_AUD`), issuer, and expiry before issuing a token (`verifyAccessJwt` in `functions/_lib/auth.js`). When `ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN`/`ACCESS_AUD` are unset it falls back to the prior header-presence check (route is still behind Access + the middleware gate). Verified with a Node WebCrypto test using a generated RSA keypair + mocked JWKS: a genuine token is accepted; forged-signature, absent, expired, wrong-aud, wrong-iss, and unknown-kid are all rejected. Full E2E needs the deployed Access env.

items.title: Cap per-trade screenshot size
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `dmAddShot` (`app/datamanager.js`) now auto-downscales (longest side ≤ 1600px) and re-encodes to JPEG q0.82 whenever an image exceeds a ~600 KB soft cap, flattening transparency first; images already compact are stored as-is, and if re-encoding doesn't shrink it the original is kept. Replaced the old "over 4 MB, store anyway?" confirm with a friendly post-add note. Verified in-browser: a 4.7 MB / 3000×2000 PNG → 1600px / ~73 KB JPEG, "Image optimized for local storage (4.7 MB → ~73 KB)" shown, 0 console errors.

items.title: Consolidate ad-hoc breakpoints into a named scale
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** consolidated the ad-hoc media breakpoints into a documented named scale — xs 460 · sm 560 · cal 620 · md 760 · lg 900, plus the wide min-width tiers 1100/1480/1700/1920 (full table commented at the top of `app/app.css`; one-line pointers in `site.css` + `index.html`). CSS media queries can't read custom properties without a build step, so the scale is a documented convention + snapped literals. Snapped the near-duplicates: `.cards` & `.mcards` 520→560, nav-hamburger 720→760, `.grid3` & `.price-grid` 880→900. `@media` conditions now use only {460,560,620,760,900} + the wide tiers — 520/720/880 no longer appear as breakpoints. items.title: (staging) Design review: per-day vs per-trade notes & data model
items.doneNote: Design review of how per-DAY and per-TRADE notes fit together. FINDINGS: per-day notes (JOURNAL store, {date,text,updated}) are plain text only, captured in a bare textarea under the calendar; per-trade notes (TRADEMETA store, {id,tags,note,shots,updated}) are rich — tags + note + downscaled screenshots — captured in a modal editor in Manage data. The asymmetry is backwards: the DAY is the natural home for a chart screenshot / morning plan / 'tilted' tag, yet only trades can hold them. Two storage shapes + two capture surfaces mean any future notes feature (search, export, tag filter) must be built twice. The 'note'-only Day-notes list in Manage data is ALREADY fixed — verified in-browser it previews date + full text (newest first); the only bare literal 'note' left is the compact per-trade presence chip (metaChips), which is intentional. RECOMMENDATION: one annotation shape {tags,text,shots}, two scopes (day|trade). Low-risk path = extend the JOURNAL record to {date,text,tags,shots,updated} (old {date,text} records read fine, fields default empty; additive export), reuse the trade editor's tag input + screenshot downscale pipeline, and replace the bare calendar textarea with a shared annotation editor (quick textarea that expands to full editor). Keep the CH11 capture-the-date autosave and the SHOT downscale pipeline. Spun off F16 (unify: give day notes tags + screenshots), CH19 (extract a shared annotation-editor component), CH20 (Manage-data Day-notes rows open/edit + consistent note·img·tag markers). No app code shipped — R3 is review-only.

items.title: Decide what business information the site needs
items.doneNote: Decided what business info the site needs and where it lives. CURRENT STATE: footer + legal.html carry good Disclaimer + Terms-of-Use + a Privacy SUMMARY + contact@blotterbook.com; but the footer has NO copyright line, the operating entity (LICENSE: 'Scaline Audio / Mitchell Guthrie') appears nowhere public, there is no formal Privacy Policy / Terms of Service / refund policy / governing-law clause, and the pricing page already advertises $25/$50 donations + a future paid add-on. OWNER DECISIONS (2026-06): governing law = Delaware; published contact = support email only (contact@blotterbook.com); paid tiers = voluntary NON-REFUNDABLE donations (no goods exchanged); operating-entity display name = TBD. CHECKLIST / PLACEMENT — Have now: not-a-broker/not-advice disclaimers (legal + footer), as-is + liability + proprietary terms (legal), privacy summary (legal), support email (footer/legal). MUST-HAVE BEFORE TAKING MONEY (gate on R15 Stripe): (1) decide + display the operating entity / seller identity (footer '© 2026 <entity>' + a 'Blotterbook is operated by <entity>' line on legal) — Stripe needs a real seller; this is the top blocker. (2) A non-refundable Donations/Refund policy on the legal page (gifts, no goods, non-refundable). (3) A governing-law/venue clause (Delaware) in the Terms. (4) Confirm the support-contact email is clearly reachable from checkout. SHOULD-HAVE soon after / before the paid synced-workspaces add-on: a fuller standalone Privacy Policy (current is a summary) and a proper Terms of Service distinct from the disclaimer bullets; billing/subscription terms when subscriptions ship. NICE-TO-HAVE: footer copyright line regardless of monetization; 'Last updated' dates per legal section. Spun off CH22 (footer copyright/operator line) and F18 (pre-monetization legal pack: ToS w/ Delaware governing law, full Privacy Policy, non-refundable Donations/Refund policy, operator identity — prereq for R15). Review-only; no app code shipped.

items.title: (staging) Debug why the Activity Terminal module won't collapse properly
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the staging Activity Terminal `.pbody` carried `display:flex` (0,3,0 specificity, same as the generic `.panel.collapsed .pbody{display:none}` but later in source, so flex won). Scoped it to `.panel.term:not(.collapsed) .pbody` (`app/app.css`). Verified: the terminal panel now collapses/expands like every other module.

items.title: Escape backup-restore symbol/tag option sinks (stored XSS)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** two layers. Sink: the f_symbol/f_tag filter <option> interpolations (app/data.js) are now wrapped in esc(). Boundary: Store.importAll() (app/store.js) forces trades[].root through Adapters.rootSym() and strips <>&"' from trademeta tags, so a restored backup matches CSV-imported data. Verified in Chromium: importing a backup whose symbol is a crafted img/onerror payload does NOT execute (no alert, no live element), the root is stored as sanitized text (EVILIMGSRCXONERROR...), the dropdown renders inert text, and a normal restore still completes.

items.title: Minor API/security hardening bundle (cookie/compare/headers)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** (1) the bb_staging admin-token cookie now carries ;Secure; (2) the raw-key fallback in isAdminAuthorized() uses a constant-time compare (HMAC both sides under a random key — leaks neither contents nor length); (3) /api/me is now no-store (via the shared json() helper); (4) dropped the wildcard Access-Control-Allow-Origin from /api/changelog. Verified with a Node WebCrypto test: constant-time accept/reject, me.js no-store, all function modules import.

items.title: Tighten CSP: drop script-src 'unsafe-inline' (externalize inline scripts)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** removed the last inline executable scripts so the CSP forbids inline JS. Moved each page's inline <script> to its own file (index->/assets/home.js, admin->/assets/admin.js, changelog->/assets/changelog.js); rewrote the main/demo new-tab Export report to load external /assets/report.js with per-report data in a non-executable <script type=application/json> block and addEventListener-wired buttons (it inherits the app CSP). _headers script-src is now 'self' (no 'unsafe-inline'); style-src keeps it (inline styles remain). Verified in Chromium UNDER THE EXACT DEPLOYED CSP HEADER: homepage feature explorer, admin backlog, the demo new-tab report (downloads a clean HTML file), and the staging export modal all work with ZERO CSP violations; no inline on*= handlers remain in served HTML.

items.title: Defense-in-depth: rate-limit admin writes + token out of localStorage
items.doneNote: **Shipped (defense-in-depth):** added a best-effort KV-backed fixed-window rate limiter (functions/_lib/http.js rateLimited()) keyed by Access email / client IP — /api/status + /api/config at 30/min, /api/admin-key at 20/min, 429 over limit; fails OPEN if STATUS_KV is unbound so it can't lock the admin out. assets/admin.js no longer writes the admin token (or any key) to localStorage — it lives only in the page-session field and autoKey() re-issues a fresh token each load, so an XSS can't lift a persisted credential. Verified with a Node test (35 reqs at limit 30 -> 5 blocked; fail-open without KV).

items.title: Commission undercount: charge per contract (× qty), not per trade
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** round-turn commission is now charged per CONTRACT — `2 × per-side rate × (qty||1)` — in `costModel()` and the curve's `dailySeries()`. Close-event exports (no qty) are unchanged; fills/MotiveWave trades with qty>1 are priced correctly. The per-symbol table gained a Contracts column so `$/RT × Cts` reconciles to Commission, and the caveat copy was updated.

items.title: Non-US date + non-ET timezone assumptions misplace trades
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `normTime()` now detects D/M/Y when the first field is >12 (e.g. 25/06 → Jun 25), defaulting to US M/D/Y otherwise; a new "US dates & Eastern time assumed" caveat documents the no-timezone-conversion / ET-session assumptions. Verified by adapter tests (M/D/Y stays US, D/M/Y day>12 swaps).

items.title: IndexedDB addTrades transaction auto-commit race on large imports
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `Store.addTrades()` (`app/store.js`) was awaiting a `get()` per trade inside one readwrite transaction, which could auto-commit mid-flight on large imports (TransactionInactiveError). It now reads all ids once into a Set, then issues every `put` in a tight synchronous loop — also dedupes rows repeated within the same batch.

items.title: Adapter parsing robustness (Schwab truncation, blank rows, flip fills)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** three adapter-robustness fixes in `app/adapters.js`: `parseCSV` drops all-empty lines; the Schwab adapter skips a row with a blank optional Side cell (`continue`) instead of `break`, only stopping at a real column-count boundary; `pairFills` apportions a flip fill's realized PnL over the closed qty (not the full fill qty) so the full realized value lands on the closed contracts. +5 regression tests (60/60 pass).

items.title: Metric display polish (recovery ∞, curve subs accrual, tax weights)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** Recovery Factor shows ∞ for an all-positive curve with no drawdown (was —); subscriptions now accrue month-by-month along the equity curve instead of dropping at day 0 (endpoint total unchanged); the 1256 tax weights are normalized if loaded `state-tax.json` drifts off the 60/40 split. In `app/core.js` + `app/render.js`.

items.title: (a11y) Data-manager modal is permanently aria-hidden; no focus management
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the data-manager and staging export modals are now accessible — a shared `modalOpened`/`modalClosed` helper (`app/ui.js`) flips `aria-hidden`, moves focus into the sheet, traps Tab, and restores focus to the opener on close. Escape + backdrop close still work. Verified in Chromium (aria-hidden toggles, focus enters/restores, Escape closes).

items.title: (a11y) Keyboard + ARIA for the feature list, calendar, and curve
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the homepage feature explorer is now a real tablist (role tab/tabpanel, roving tabindex, Arrow/Home/End with selection-follows-focus, aria-live detail); calendar day cells and the equity-curve SVG are keyboard-operable (tabindex/role=button, Enter/Space; the curve adds arrow-key date navigation), and decorative `.ficon` SVGs are `aria-hidden`. Verified in Chromium (15 keyboard/ARIA assertions).

items.title: Add social/SEO meta (OG, Twitter card, canonical) + OG image
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** generated a 1200x630 raster OG card (assets/og-image.png, branded dark card). index.html gets full Open Graph + Twitter summary_large_image tags + rel=canonical; howto/roadmap/changelog/legal each get rel=canonical + per-page OG/Twitter (own title/description, shared OG image). Shared links now render a rich preview and every public page declares a canonical URL.

items.title: Add robots.txt + sitemap.xml
items.doneNote: **Audit + shipped.** A Cloudflare-managed robots.txt was already served at the edge (AI Crawl Control: `User-agent:* / Allow:/` + `Content-Signal: search=yes,ai-train=no`, and `Disallow:/` for Amazonbot, Applebot-Extended, Bytespider, CCBot, ClaudeBot, CloudflareBrowserRenderingCrawler, Google-Extended, GPTBot, meta-externalagent; no sitemap). Per decision, that managed file was DISABLED in the Cloudflare dashboard so there's one source of truth. Added a committed root `robots.txt` that subsumes the prior AI-crawler blocks + the `Content-Signal` reservation, and adds `Disallow: /app/` + `/admin.html` and a `Sitemap:` line; plus `sitemap.xml` listing the 5 public pages (matching their CH5 canonical URLs). Verified: sitemap is valid XML and its locs equal the page canonicals; robots carries the disallows + content-signal + sitemap line. NOTE: confirm on the live site post-deploy that only our file serves (the Cloudflare override is off).

items.title: Add CI/automation so the hand-run build tooling can't drift
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** .github/workflows/ci.yml (push to main + PRs) runs test-adapters.cjs, regenerates partials (build-includes) + the data manifest (build-manifest), and FAILS on any uncommitted diff — so stale partials or a stale manifest can't merge. Made build-manifest deterministic for the gate: dropped the generatedAt timestamp (nothing reads it; the app cache-busts the manifest fetch itself) and excluded backlog.json (admin-only, fetched no-store) so the manifest stops churning on backlog edits. Verified: manifest re-run = no diff, 55/55 adapter tests pass, build-includes idempotent. Note: a SessionStart hook isn't committable (.claude/ is gitignored) — the Actions workflow is the enforcement.

items.title: Single-source the version string (kill the staging.js literal + drift)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** app/staging.js reads the version from the .ver page badge instead of a hardcoded 'v0.13' in the activity-terminal 'session ready' line, so a bump no longer touches staging.js. README documents a version-bump checklist (badge = display source; reconcile config.js KV defaults + admin placeholder). Verified: the staging terminal shows 'Staging session ready · v0.13' read from the badge.

items.title: Small cleanups: stale CSS breakpoint comment + relative live-pill fetch
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the app/app.css L1 breakpoint-scale comment now references surviving selectors (.uc-row, .feat-explorer) instead of the F7-deleted .grid3/.uc-grid; assets/home.js live-status pill pings /app/ (absolute) instead of the relative app/, matching every other fetch + the _redirects convention.

items.title: Extract the duplicated functions json() Response helper
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** new functions/_lib/http.js exports a single json(obj,status,headers) helper (no-store + charset by default). config/status/admin-key dropped their byte-identical local copies; me/checkout/webhook/geo now return via the shared helper for a consistent response shape (also gives /api/me no-store). changelog keeps its bespoke long-cache headers by design. Verified: all function modules import; json() defaults + override correct.

items.title: Finish A5's deferred tail: app-topbar + app-scripts partials, demo shell
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** finished A5's tail — `app-topbar` and `app-scripts` partials now single-source the three top bars and the ordering-sensitive script block, and demo's trimmed landing + preview-only modal fold into `app-landing`/`app-modal` via a `mode=demo` branch. The three built pages are render-equivalent to before (comment-stripped/whitespace-normalized diff + 27 Chromium assertions); build stays idempotent and 100% static.

items.title: Theming hygiene: route stray hexes through tokens + AA contrast check
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** stray hex literals routed through `tokens.css` — added `--hover-line` (#33405a hover borders) and replaced on-accent text `#0d1014` with `var(--bg)`. `--faint` was failing WCAG AA on `--bg` (3.18:1) and was bumped #5b6470 → #767f8c (4.71:1); `--dim` (6.22:1) already passed.

items.title: Large-import crash: Math.max(...pnls) spread overflows the call stack
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `compute()` (`app/core.js`) now tracks best/worst as running min/max inside the existing equity-curve loop instead of `Math.max(...pnls)`/`Math.min(...pnls)` — the spread overflowed the call stack on large fills exports and blanked the dashboard. Verified a 200k-row dataset no longer throws; metrics unchanged.

items.title: Unguarded top-level prev/next handlers can kill app boot
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the calendar prev/next handlers route through the null-safe `on()` helper (`app/main.js`) instead of `$('prev').onclick`, so an absent id can't throw at module load and kill `boot()`. Verified prev/next still navigate months.

items.title: Cost inputs silently coerce blank/NaN to $0, understating costs
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `numIn()` (`app/core.js`) clamps negatives to 0 (a typed `-5` can't manufacture negative cost) and the platform-fee input gained `placeholder="0"` so an empty field reads as 0 rather than a typed value.

items.title: TradeStation auto-detect matches at the acceptance floor (over-eager)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the TradeStation adapter (`app/adapters.js`) now requires the distinctive combined `date/time` column and scores 3 (was 2, the detect floor), so a generic split-time fills export no longer auto-claims as TradeStation. IBKR also has date/time but uses `buy/sell`, so no collision. Regression test added (62/62 pass).

items.title: Keyboard focus lost on calendar/curve re-render (B10 follow-on)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `renderCalendar()` (`app/render.js`) remembers the focused day's date and restores focus to the same cell after its wholesale innerHTML rebuild, so keyboard users no longer drop to `<body>` on re-render. (The `#curve` SVG element persists across renders, so it never lost focus.) Verified in Chromium.

items.title: Admin auth / staging gate / ?check fail-open when Access env unset
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** admin-key issuance and the staging gate now **fail closed** when `ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN`/`ACCESS_AUD` are unset (admin-key → 503, staging → 403) unless `ALLOW_PRESENCE_AUTH=1` is set for local/preview — closing the spoof-the-header-off-Access path. The `?check` diagnostic is gated behind `ADMIN_DEBUG=1` (off by default) so it can't disclose Access config/email to anonymous callers. Prod (Access configured) is unaffected. README updated.

items.title: Pre-implementation security checklist for the Stripe/account stubs
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** added `verifyStripeSignature()` (`functions/_lib/auth.js`) — HMAC-SHA256 over the RAW body, constant-time compare, 5-minute replay window, multi-`v1` rotation support — and wired `/api/webhook` to verify before acting (no secret → 501, bad sig → 400, valid → 501 pending provisioning). Reinforced checkout/me with SECURITY notes (server-side price IDs only; authorize the session before a non-local tier). New `scripts/test-auth.mjs` (13 assertions) added to CI. Account provisioning itself stays a scaffold.

items.title: Single-source the HTML-escape helper and make it quote-safe
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the three duplicated escapers (`esc` in datamanager.js used app-wide, `esc` in changelog.js, `bkesc` in admin.js) collapse into one shared `assets/util.js` loaded first on every page. The shared `esc()` now also encodes `"` and `'`, closing the latent attribute-injection that only the strict CSP was guarding. Verified across app/changelog/admin.

items.title: Exported report hardcodes the palette (drifted from tokens.css)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the exported report's `:root` is generated from the live token values (`getComputedStyle` at export time) instead of a hardcoded palette, and the remaining `#0d1014` literals read from `var(--bg)`/the `--bg` token. The report no longer ships the pre-L5 `--faint:#5b6470` — verified it now carries `#767f8c`, matching tokens.css.

items.title: Fold the last hand-duplicated nav/footer into the include/token system
items.doneNote: **Shipped (documented divergence):** index.html (marketing landing) and admin.html (internal tool) keep genuinely-distinct bespoke nav/footer — homepage uses section anchors + CTA/hamburger and a fuller disclaimer; admin uses a trimmed nav + 'internal' disclaimer. Folding them into the shared partials (or coupling the standalone homepage to site.css) would add risk for no gain, so each file now carries an inline note explaining why it stays bespoke, and the build-includes.mjs header is corrected (it also fixes the now-stale claim that demo keeps a bespoke shell — A6 folded it in).

items.title: Small refactor/perf cleanups surfaced by the R1 audit
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `costModel(m)` computed once per `renderDash()` and threaded into renderCards/renderAdv/renderCalc (was ~4 walks/render); `downloadFile()` now takes a string OR a Blob (replacing `expDlBlob`); the report's headline figures are defined once and rendered as both HTML tiles and the plaintext summary; journal autosave captures date+text at schedule time (no wrong-date writes on a fast day-switch); stale `var(--red,#e0564d)`/`var(--panel,#11151b)` CSS fallbacks removed; and the CI drift gate now also catches untracked outputs via `git status --porcelain`.

items.title: (staging) Restyle the 1256 tax row as a collapsible breakdown
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** in staging, the Break-even module now shows one **Est. 1256 tax (net profit only)** headline line with the total, and the State top rate + Blended 1256 rate moved into a collapsible **Tax breakdown** details element (mirrors the F6 commission subsection). Main app + demo keep the inline rows. Values unchanged (gated on STAGING_PAGE in renderCalc).

items.title: Day-notes editor visible only when a day is selected; click-outside deselects
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the Day-notes editor (#journal) is hidden until a day is actively selected and reappears on selection; a click outside the Trading Calendar module (and outside the equity curve, the other date-selection surface) deselects the day — clears the highlight, drops the graph marker, hides the box. Capture-phase document handler so a curve re-render cannot detach the target. Verified on app/demo/staging.

items.title: (staging) Reverse the Activity Terminal so newest is on top
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the staging Activity Terminal now prepends new lines so the most recent entry is at the TOP; the line cap trims the oldest (now last) and the view scrolls to top. Formatting/cap unchanged.

items.title: Automate two-track versioning from conventional commits + merges
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** one source of truth data/versions.json ({prod, staging}) replaces the two disconnected sources. A CI job (.github/workflows/version-bump.yml -> scripts/bump-version.mjs) bumps on merge to main — level from the conventional-commit type, track from changed paths (shared code -> both, app/staging.* -> staging, non-app -> none) — and commits versions.json back as chore(release): ... [skip ci]. Badges are runtime-fetched (assets/util.js) with the baked partial literal as offline fallback; /api/config + admin show the values read-only (manual inputs + KV versions record retired); phase derived from prod major (0.x -> Beta). versions.json excluded from the manifest; separate concerns (DB_VERSION, backup version, manifest hashes, refDataVersion) untouched. 24 bump-logic unit tests + 7 Chromium assertions pass; added to CI. README versioning section rewritten. Seed prod 0.12.0 / staging 0.22.0.

items.title: Drop prompts from Done items; keep only doneNote
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** stripped the `prompt` field from all 74 done items (kept only `doneNote`, the shipped record), and added a top-level `donePolicy` next to `commitConvention` stating that setting an item to `done` removes its prompt and keeps the doneNote (prompts stay on open/guardrail items). Net diff: 1 insertion (the policy) + 74 prompt deletions, no other changes. Verified backlog.json is valid JSON and the admin backlog panel still renders all 87 items (74 done · 11 open · 2 guardrail) with no errors — it never rendered done-item prompts anyway.

items.title: Revamp the changelog into versioned, user-facing release notes
items.doneNote: Discussed and agreed three decisions (curated source / prod track only / manual maintenance), then built. New source of truth: curated, version-keyed data/changelog.json (prod track, newest first) — each release has version, date, friendly title/summary, optional highlights; pre-versioning rolled into one beta:true 'Beta released' entry. assets/changelog.js now fetches the static file and renders versioned timeline cards (version badge, formatted date, Latest pill, summary, highlight bullets) with a small inline fallback; retired the raw-commit functions/api/changelog.js entirely. Seeded from the real chore(release) history: 0.13.0→0.14.2 plus the Beta floor. Added .entry .ver/.summary/.highlights styles to site.css; excluded changelog.json from build-manifest (fetched directly, like backlog/versions). Updated README, legal.html, and changelog.html copy off 'commit history'. Verified in-browser: 5 entries render from the live fetch, no console errors; tests 62/27/13 green, builds idempotent. Future releases: add a top entry to data/changelog.json each prod bump.

items.title: Define the staging → app/demo promotion workflow
items.doneNote: **Discussed, then documented.** Agreed three decisions: (1) version model stays independent counters — a promotion bumps prod by the commit-type level and staging keeps its lead, no numeric sync; (2) lives in README as a section + copy-pasteable checklist; (3) demo mirrors prod 1:1, with trade-mutating UI greyed out/disabled and persistence blocked (DEMO_MODE), so a promoted feature MUST appear on demo with its restrictions applied, not be excluded. Added a 'Promoting a feature from staging → prod' subsection to README.md: the surface-by-gate model, the four gate layers (staging.js / STAGING_PAGE runtime guard / inline staging HTML / mode=staging partial conditional) and how to un-gate each, that staging is a proving ground where EVERY feature promotes (the activity terminal, session pill, and workspace templates are features, not dev tools), and an 8-step checklist covering gate removal, demo-restriction preservation, build-includes regeneration, three-surface verification, conventional-commit titling for the CH12 bump, and a changelog (F13) entry.

items.title: (staging) Make the 5 headline stat cards open metric detail modals
items.doneNote: Built staging-only (gate on STAGING_PAGE) per the proving-ground model. renderCards (app/render.js) makes the five headline cards role=button/tabindex=0/data-card with an aria-label only on staging; app/demo cards stay plain. A single reusable #cardModal shell (partials/app-modal.html, <!--IF mode=staging-->) is populated per metric by widgets.js openCardModal() + CARD_VIEWS, with inline-SVG helpers (cmCurve/cmHist/cmBars/cmSplit) and aggregators (cmDow/cmSymPf/cmDDMarks) over the active compute() metrics — no new persistence. Net PnL → equity curve + gross→take-home component bars; Win Rate → W/L/BE split + by-weekday + long/short; Profit Factor → gross-profit vs gross-loss + per-symbol PF table; Avg Win/Loss → win & loss distribution histograms; Max Drawdown → equity curve with peak/trough markers. Reuses the .modal/.sheet pattern + B9 modalOpened/modalClosed focus trap; cards are keyboard-operable (Enter/Space, B10) and Escape/click-outside/✕ close. Verified: all five open metric-specific modals with focus moving into the dialog, keyboard open/close works, and app/demo cards remain non-interactive (no data-card, no #cardModal).

items.title: (staging) Show the collapse control only when a module is full-width
items.doneNote: Built all-surfaces via a pure CSS media query (no JS) — the web-grid is universal since CH16, so gating to staging would have re-added the body[data-mode=staging] CSS that CH16 removed. In app/app.css's ≥1100px grid block, the chevron is hidden for every panel except the full-width Performance panel, and a non-perf .collapsed panel's body is forced visible (so a panel collapsed on a narrow screen can't get stuck collapsed-with-no-control after the window widens; the term panel keeps its flex body). Below 1100px every panel is single-column/full-width and stays collapsible exactly as before. Verified in-browser: at 1400px the calendar chevron computes display:none while Performance's shows; at 820px the calendar chevron returns.

items.title: F11: clicking inside a modal deselects the calendar day
items.doneNote: app/main.js capture-phase deselect guard now also early-returns on `e.target.closest('.modal')`, so interacting with the data-manager or export modal keeps the selected day. deselectDay() (app/data.js) flushes the debounced journal note (clearTimeout(jSaveTimer) + Store.saveJournal) before nulling selectedDate, closing the data-loss window.

items.title: Admin token leaks via the ?k= staging-launch URL (Referer/history)
items.doneNote: Kept ?k= as the navigation-safe fallback but neutered its exposure: a top-of-file IIFE in assets/util.js (loads first on every page) history.replaceState-strips ?k= immediately on load, and app/staging.html now carries <meta name="referrer" content="no-referrer"> so no subresource fetch leaks the token via Referer. Token no longer lingers in the address bar/history or any Referer header.

items.title: Harden the version-bump workflow (race, merge commits, edge cases)
items.doneNote: Hardened both halves of the CH12 automation. version-bump.yml: added `concurrency: {group: version-bump-main, cancel-in-progress: false}`, fetch-depth:0, diffs the pushed range (before..after with a HEAD~1 fallback), and wraps bump→build-includes→commit→push in a 5-attempt retry loop (reset --hard HEAD~1 + pull --rebase on rejection) so close merges can't lose a bump. bump-version.mjs: the local `git diff HEAD~1 HEAD` is now guarded (try/catch → treat as no changes) so it's safe on the first/shallow commit. util.js sets window.__versionsReady = Promise.resolve(null) before its early return so the promise contract always holds.

items.title: Prod version never bumps on homepage / info-page changes
items.doneNote: Chose the 'what users see at blotterbook.com' intent. Added a PROD_ONLY set (index.html, site.css, howto/roadmap/legal/changelog.html) to classifySurfaces in scripts/bump-version.mjs so those user-facing changes now bump the prod track. Added prod-only surface cases to test-version.mjs (now 27 tests): index.html → prod only, site.css+howto.html → prod only, admin.html/README/backlog → neither, index.html+staging.js → both.

items.title: Baked .ver fallback literals drift behind the live version
items.doneNote: bump-version.mjs now calls syncBakedBadges() after writing versions.json: it regex-rewrites the baked `.ver` literals in partials/app-topbar.html (Main app/Demo → prod, Staging → staging), and the workflow runs build-includes and commits partials/app-topbar.html + app/app.html/demo.html/staging.html alongside data/versions.json. The offline fallback now tracks the live version. Synced the current literals to v0.14.1 (Main/Demo) / v0.24.1 (Staging).

items.title: Imported screenshot data URLs are unsanitized at the sink
items.doneNote: Added a cleanShots() sanitizer to importAll (app/store.js) that keeps only strings matching `^data:image/(png|jpe?g|webp|gif);base64,[A-Za-z0-9+/=]+$`, dropping any crafted shot payload at the trust boundary. Defense-in-depth: the editor now escapes the value at the render site — `` (app/datamanager.js). A malformed shot URL is now both dropped on import and inert if rendered.

The Feature flags section already carried the '(Scaffold — not yet consumed by the main app.)' note; reworded the Reference data section in admin.html to state plainly that the control only records a server-side timestamp in KV and has no client effect — clients cache-bust reference data via per-file hashes in data/manifest.json, not refDataVersion. Added a 'MIRROR of platformLabel() in scripts/bump-version.mjs — keep in sync' note on the util.js copy so the Node/CI side and browser side stay aligned.

items.doneNote: Mapped the undefined CSS tokens in app/app.css's promoted widget blocks to the canonical ones — --muted→--dim, --text→--txt, --field→--panel2 (dropped the #10141a literal fallback). Verified in-browser: the version badge and session pill resolve to #8a94a3 (--dim) and the workspace select background to #1b212a (--panel2); `grep -nE 'var\(--(muted|text|field)' app/app.css` returns nothing.

items.title: Stale 'staging-only / staging.js' comments after the CH16 promotion
items.doneNote: Updated the stale post-CH16 comments that referenced the deleted app/staging.js or labelled promoted features 'staging-only': app/ui.js:5, app/main.js:132 (app:ready) + :97 (saved-filter controls), app/core.js event-bus block, app/data.js:207/:212 (saved filters), app/datamanager.js:68. items.title: Escape two admin/innerHTML sinks; sandbox the export-report iframe

items.title: Review & plan: repo/folder structure for maintainability
items.doneNote: Reviewed the repo/folder structure against best practice for a build-stepless Cloudflare Pages static site. KEY FINDING: because there is NO build step, the repo root IS the deploy/web root — the folder layout is simultaneously the public URL structure AND the deploy contract (measured coupling: 23 absolute /app/ refs, 19 /assets/ refs, /data/*.json + /api/* fetches, _redirects/_headers/robots.txt/sitemap.xml + page canonicals, and hardcoded path prefixes in build-includes ['.', 'app'], build-manifest data/* filename excludes, and bump-version's prod-shipping classifier). So the textbook '/src vs /public with a bundler' reorg FIGHTS the architecture: it either changes every public URL (canonical/sitemap/_redirects/bookmark churn) or requires a build/copy step that violates the 'no build step / deploys as static files' design pillar. CONCLUSION: keep the current root=web-root layout — it is the idiomatic, correct structure for this deploy model (functions/ pinned at root as Pages requires; _headers/_redirects/robots/sitemap/favicon at root; concerns co-located in app/ partials/ data/ assets/ scripts/ functions/; tokens+partials already single-sourced via A1/A5/A6). CONSIDERED & DECLINED as not worth the churn: scripts/->tests/ split (cosmetic; CI path coupling), assets/ -> img+js split (changes ~19 URLs + favicon/OG/_headers refs), data/ subgrouping (breaks fetch URLs + manifest keys), marketing pages -> /site (SEO/canonical/sitemap churn). The ONE Pages-compatible reorg, should the root ever genuinely need it, is a public/ output-dir split (browser-served files under public/, Pages build-output-directory=public, keep functions/+scripts/+partials/+tooling at root) — URLs stay identical, but _headers/_redirects relocate into public/ and every build-script path prefix changes; deferred (cost > payoff for a small project now). SPUN OFF: A18 (guardrail — repo layout is the deploy contract; reject a /src+bundler reorg, and if ever reorganizing use the public/ output-dir approach + update the full coupled-path set in lockstep) and CH28 (document the build-stepless layout contract + path-coupling map in docs/architecture.md). Review-only; no code shipped.

items.title: Document the build-stepless layout contract + path-coupling map (docs/architecture.md)
items.doneNote: Documented the build-stepless layout contract. Added a 'Repository layout & the deploy contract' section to docs/architecture.md (after Design pillars; registered in the Contents list): no build step -> the repo root IS the Cloudflare Pages web root -> a file's path IS its public URL. Lists what Pages pins at the deploy root (functions/, _headers, _redirects, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, favicon, and the /-serving pages) and a coupled-path table that must change in lockstep if any browser-served file moves: the ~23 /app/ + ~19 /assets/ absolute refs and /data//api fetches, _redirects, _headers (CSP connect-src), robots/sitemap + page canonicals (CH5/CH6), build-includes.mjs (['.', 'app'] scan + partials/), build-manifest.mjs (data/* hashing + filename excludes), and bump-version.mjs (prod-shipping path classifier). States: do NOT reorganize into src/+public/ with a bundler (violates the no-build pillar); the only Pages-compatible reorg is a public/ output-dir split. CLAUDE.md's Repo layout section gained a blockquote summary + link to the new section (refs guardrail A18). Docs only; no code/build impact.

items.title: Move dynamic styling out of inline style= into CSS classes + custom properties
items.doneNote: Moved dynamic styling out of inline style= into CSS classes + custom properties (set via the CSSOM), eliminating all runtime data-driven style= from the chart/bar renderers. Converted: the equity-curve gradient stops -> SVG presentation attributes (stop-color/stop-opacity); the curve end-labels -> class .endlab + --c via CSSOM, with a .curve text.endlab{fill:var(--c)} rule that beats the blanket .curve text{fill:var(--dim)} (a bare fill= attribute would have LOST to it — the specificity-inversion trap, caught + handled); the curve hover-tooltip swatch, the card-modal bars (cmBars .cmbf + cmSplit .cmseg), and the admin backlog bars (.bkbar i) -> class + data-w/data-c with a post-insert CSSOM pass (el.style.setProperty('--w'/'--c')) at each render site. Added matching var(--w)/var(--c) CSS in app/app.css + admin.html. VERIFIED zero visual regression: headless-Chromium before/after screenshots of the demo dashboard (curve gradient + end-labels), the curve tooltip, the Net PnL modal (cmBars waterfall), the Win Rate modal (cmSplit), and the admin backlog bars are BYTE-IDENTICAL; node --check + full test suite + build-drift gate green. DEFERRED (partial — out of the chart/bar-renderer done-when, lower value + higher cost): the ~23 static style= attributes in committed HTML, the few static style= in data.js/datamanager.js/main.js, and the large inline <style> blocks in index.html/admin.html (externalizing those adds FOUC + reverses A9 + a new CSS-file URL under the deploy contract — better paired with the R19 build decision). Also a prerequisite step toward dropping style-src 'unsafe-inline' later (currently shelved).

items.title: Targeted DOM updates on interactive surfaces (retire wholesale innerHTML re-render)
items.doneNote: Verified ALREADY SATISFIED — no code change needed. The prompt's premise (the interactive renderers 'rebuild whole subtrees via innerHTML on every change') was an overstatement; prior incremental work already implemented targeted updates. Verified via Chromium DevTools-Protocol interaction tests on the live app: (1) CALENDAR selection is already targeted — selectDay/deselectDay (app/data.js) toggle the .selday class on the specific cell and persistDay toggles .hasnote on one cell, with NO renderCalendar() rebuild; CDP test confirmed focusing cell X then selecting cell Y keeps focus on X, leaves the grid DOM-node identity unchanged (no rebuild), and gives Y .selday. (2) CURVE keeps interactive state — the #curve SVG element persists across renders (only its innerHTML is replaced); CDP test confirmed focusing the curve then toggling a Gross/Net/Take-home overlay (a full renderCurve) keeps focus on the same SVG node. (3) DAY-NOTE editor edits survive re-render — code-verified that no re-render path touches the note textarea: renderDash -> renderDayTrades writes only #j_trades (a separate container), renderCalendar only toggles #journal's display, and updateJournalEditor (which does rebuild #j_note) runs only on an intentional day-switch / data-load, where CH11's capture-snapshot persists the outgoing day first. (Not runtime-tested on demo since note editing is disabled there, but the render/editor separation is unambiguous in code.) The remaining wholesale innerHTML rebuilds (full renderCalendar grid, renderCurve svg) happen only on genuine DATA/MONTH/OVERLAY changes where a rebuild is correct. The B15 focus-restore in renderCalendar therefore STAYS — it still guards the genuine same-month data-reload rebuild (import/delete) and isn't removable without a riskier grid-diffing rebuild that isn't worth it. Behavioral done-when met with no change; the 'remove B15' sub-clause rested on the inaccurate premise and is intentionally left as-is. Closed like L7/CH3 (verified-resolved).

items.title: Migrate to native ES modules (kill global-scope + fixed-load-order coupling)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the app and info-page scripts now run as native ES modules (`<script type="module">`) with explicit import/export — no shared global scope, no fixed load order, and NO build step or dependencies added. `partials/app-scripts.html` collapsed from 11 ordered classic `<script>`s to a single module entry `<script type="module" src="main.js">` (main.js imports the rest; `widgets.js` is a side-effect import so its event-bus subscriptions register before boot() emits app:ready). Each module exports what others use and imports what it needs. Reassignable cross-module state (TRADES, METRICS_ALL, SCOPE, calYear/calMonth, selectedDate, JOURNAL_DATES, TRADE_META, DEMO_MODE, PENDING, FILE_CTX, SAVED_FILTERS, STAGING_DATA_READY, DM_SEARCH, DM_EDIT) moved to a shared `app/state.js` object (`state.X`) since ESM import bindings are read-only; the const objects FILTERS/curveSel stay plain exports. `assets/util.js` is now a module imported by both the app and the info pages (changelog/admin), exposing `esc`/`platformLabel`/`versionsReady` (replacing `window.__versionsReady`); the DOM helper `on` moved into core.js. `app/adapters.js` became a real ESM module (named + default export); its Node test was renamed `scripts/test-adapters.cjs` → `.mjs` (import instead of require, since CI Node 20 cannot require ESM) with `.github/workflows/ci.yml` + CLAUDE.md updated. The unloaded `app/entitlements.js` scaffold was converted too. Verified headlessly: app/demo/staging + index/changelog/admin all boot with ZERO console/page errors, and demo/staging interactions (stat-card detail modals, calendar day selection, data manager, per-trade tag editor → persisted to Store and reloaded, export-report modal, scope/overlay toggles, workspace reset) all run clean; full test suite green (adapters 72, auth 13, version 28, flags 5, tax 6) and the build-includes/build-manifest drift gate is clean.

A 'build' splits into Tier A (tests/lint/format that never touch shipped bytes — keeps the deploy contract) and Tier B (bundler/minify/hashed names/nonce-CSP that reverses it); Tier A delivers ~80% of the value (esp. the render-test gap) for ~10% of the cost. SHIPPED: package.json (private) + lockfile with devDependencies only (eslint+@eslint/js+globals flat config, prettier, @playwright/test pinned to the pre-installed chromium); committed Playwright render/E2E tests (every surface boots with zero console errors + interaction/persistence smoke — closes the render-test gap); ESLint clean (fixed 4 real findings in adapters.js); Prettier wired but repo-wide reformat deferred (CH32) to avoid a destructive diff; CI runs npm ci → lint → unit tests → Playwright → the drift gate (which also proves tooling never writes into shipped files); node_modules/playwright-report/test-results gitignored. DEFERRED/FILED: A24 (Tier B shipped-output build, gated on the S18 'drop unsafe-inline' trigger or a measured perf problem), A25 (guardrail: dev tooling stays dev-only), CH32 (Prettier repo-wide reformat). NOTE for ops: set SKIP_DEPENDENCY_INSTALL=1 on Cloudflare Pages so devDeps never run at deploy.

items.title: Finish S18: CSP style-src 'self' (drop unsafe-inline)
items.doneNote: S18 complete — _headers CSP flipped to style-src 'self' (dropped 'unsafe-inline'). (1) Added app/staging-svelte/actions.js with a styleProps use:action that applies dynamic styles via element.style.setProperty() (CSSOM — NOT gated by style-src); converted EquityCurve (--sw swatch + fill end-label) and StatCardModal (.seg flex/background + two .fill width/background) to it. (2) report.js reportHtmlDoc now returns {html, css} with NO inline <style>; ExportReport.svelte injects the CSS via a constructable stylesheet (adoptedStyleSheets) on iframe load, and the PNG/JPEG rasterizer embeds the same CSS string in its SVG foreignObject (image context, not page style-src). (3) Extracted the static inline <style> blocks on the marketing/admin pages into linked CSS — index.html→home.css (NEW), admin.html→admin.css (NEW); the few remaining style= attrs (soon-badge, year amount, admin dots, howto narrow field) became utility classes in home.css/admin.css/site.css. (4) Flipped _headers to style-src 'self' and updated its comment block. Verified: built dist HTML has ZERO inline <style>/style= (grep), and a Playwright CSP-injection check (route adds the prod CSP header, listens for securitypolicyviolation) across index/admin/howto/app/demo/staging — exercising the styleProps overlays, stat-card modal, and export-report iframe — reported NONE. build + npm test (lint/typecheck/format/unit 34) + e2e (14) + drift gate all green. — A128 CLARIFICATION (2026-06-30): the Tailwind v4 + shadcn-svelte/bits-ui adoption does NOT weaken this. Tailwind emits a single linked stylesheet of utility CLASSES (class="bg-accent"), never an inline style="" attribute, so style-src 'self' still holds; bits-ui/Floating-UI positions popovers/menus by setting element.style in JS (CSSOM — the same mechanism as styleProps, NOT gated by style-src). The S18 invariant is unchanged: no literal style="" attribute in markup (utilities/styleProps do dynamic styling). The CSP-injection e2e check still passes across every surface.

items.doneNote: Done — analyzed and decided NOT to adopt SvelteKit; staying on the Vite multi-page + Svelte 5 SPA (ADR-001). Of SvelteKit's headline features, SSR + form actions are non-features for a local-only app, SSG + endpoints are already covered (Vite MPA static marketing pages; the functions/* edge layer), routing is a future micro-dep if ever needed, and adapter-cloudflare only exists to serve the rest. Its load/server idiom is a standing hazard to the HARD no-trade-data-leaves-the-browser pillar (the server must never see trades), and adopting it would re-open the just-stabilized A18/A26 deploy contract and churn the security-reviewed functions/* layer for zero capability gain. Owner reviewed and confirmed: not converting, not revisiting — so the written analysis doc was reverted rather than kept. Sequencing left intact: A69 (Svelte-ify marketing) proceeds with plain Svelte under the Vite MPA build, A30 (reorg) on the Vite/Svelte structure, A61 (TS) orthogonal.
items.title: Discuss: adopt SvelteKit (pros/cons + analysis)

items.doneNote: Done — read-only structure audit + decided target layout delivered in docs/structure-reorg-plan.md. Findings: the flat root-as-web-root layout no longer earns its keep (A26 decoupled source from URL; no surface is servable un-built), so the A30 reorg is justified as a P3 maintainability cleanup. Catalogued the pure-logic core, the mixed assets/ dir (bundled JS+SVG vs the one verbatim-static og-image.png), the misplaced/colliding util.js files (assets/util.js shared-core vs app/staging-svelte/util.js app-only), the app/staging-svelte misnomer, app/entitlements.js as intentional-but-currently-dead scaffold, and the full coupled-path set (vite/build-manifest/bump-version/build-includes/copy-static/jsconfig/eslint/prettier + imports). Per the owner's call, designed ONE target layout (src/{lib,app,site} + static/ publicDir; functions/ pinned at root) that ABSORBS A69 so A30 lands once and is final: src/site/ has prepared homes for the Svelte marketing components + shared chrome, retiring partials/ + build-includes; static/ replaces copy-static.mjs. Recommendation: A61(TS) decision first, then A69+A30 as one coordinated reorg-and-convert pass. Follow-ups: A30 + A69 prompts repointed at the plan; filed A76 (util.js collision + entitlements.js scaffold cleanup, rides with A30).
items.title: Audit project structure to optimize for Vite + Svelte

items.doneNote: Done — discussion + decision delivered in docs/typescript-decision.md. Owner decided: ADOPT full native TypeScript everywhere (incl. the pure-logic core), strict from the start, with the .js→.ts / lang=ts renames FOLDED INTO the A30 reorg (touch files once). Analysis covered what TS buys beyond today's JSDoc @ts-check (biggest win = real types on the 18 untyped Svelte components; ergonomics + one language elsewhere; marginal added safety on the already-@ts-check'd core), and the mechanics/risks: Vite/esbuild compiles .ts free at build; the node test suites can't run .ts raw → use Node 22 native --experimental-strip-types (zero dep, preferred) or tsx fallback; svelte-check (dev-only) added for component typing; jsconfig→tsconfig strict; functions/ convertible separately. A29 reconciled: JS→TS is a behavior-preserving syntax-host change (no logic rewrite), gated by the node suites passing identically. Follow-ups: A30 prompt updated to fold in the full-TS conversion; A29 guardrail amended with the TS clarification; filed A77 (Phase-1 toolchain scaffold pre-step, P2) and A78 (Phase-3 functions→TS, P3).
items.title: Discuss: migrate from JS to TypeScript

Added svelte-check (dev-only, pinned 4.3.3) + tsconfig.svelte.json; `npm run typecheck` now runs tsc + svelte-check, so the 18 Svelte components are type-checked for the first time (0 errors, 5 warnings). Proved the .ts test runner: Node 22.22 runs/imports .ts with type-stripping ON BY DEFAULT (no flag, zero dep). Verified npm test green.

items.title: (infra) Cache the per-visitor /api/* calls to extend the Workers free-tier headroom
items.doneNote: **Shipped (infra).** Edge-cached the rarely-changing public GETs to cut the per-visitor Function invocations + KV reads that count against the Workers free-tier cap (and the A17 KV budget). Added `cachedJson()` / `purgeCached()` helpers (functions/_lib/http.ts) over the colo Cache API (`caches.default`) + browser `Cache-Control`: GET /api/status edge-cached 30s, GET /api/config 60s (both visitor-independent; a brief stale read is harmless and an admin POST purges the entry so changes apply immediately); GET /api/geo gets `Cache-Control: private, max-age=1800` (the response is the visitor's OWN coarse region, so browser-only caching — no shared cache can hand one visitor's region to another). Admin POST writes stay `no-store` (json() default), rate-limited and auth-gated — never cached. All cache ops are best-effort/fail-safe (try/catch → fall through to a fresh build), and each endpoint already degrades gracefully when KV/cf is unbound (status→auto, config→APP_FLAGS defaults, geo→null prefill). Before: every visit fired ~1–3 uncached Function calls (homepage status pill, app-boot config, landing geo), each doing a KV read; after: status/config serve from the colo cache (skipping the KV read) and all three are browser-cacheable, so a session's repeat reads don't re-invoke. typecheck (incl tsc functions) + eslint (.ts linted since A79) + the 16 auth tests green. (Edge behavior verifies on the Cloudflare deploy — no local Workers runtime.)

items.title: Extract a shared Modal.svelte wrapper (overlay/card/head/close duplicated)
items.doneNote: **Resolved by investigation (R1 follow-up, #64) — not pursued.** Extracting a shared Modal.svelte does NOT cleanly apply to this scoped-CSS Svelte codebase: the three modal cards genuinely differ — StatCardModal is overflow:auto (whole card scrolls, max-width 460px) while ManageData/ExportReport are display:flex/column/overflow:hidden with an internally-scrolling body (960px/880px); overlay padding (6vh/4vh/5vh) and z-index (60/50/60) differ, and ManageData's use:modal has a custom close (cancel edit, else close). The only genuinely-shared surface is the backdrop + dialog a11y wiring (role/aria/use:modal/click-outside) + the × glyph (~18 lines); parameterising the real differences yields more indirection than dedup and risks layout regressions e2e won't catch. Closed as won't-do; revisit only as part of a deliberate modal redesign.

items.title: Single shared `.pos`/`.neg` tone rule (redefined in 7 components)
items.doneNote: **Resolved by investigation (R1 follow-up, #64) — not pursued.** A single shared .pos/.neg tone rule is unsafe here: a blanket :global(.pos){color:var(--green)} loses to each component's scoped .x.pos rule (Svelte's scope-hash raises specificity above a bare global), so it does nothing without !important. And 'pos'/'neg' is overloaded — most components use it for TEXT color, but CalendarMonth uses .cell.pos/.neg for a BACKGROUND tint, which a global text-color rule would bleed onto. A safe consolidation would require renaming the calendar's background classes AND raising the global's specificity — more churn than the P3 dedup is worth. Closed as won't-do; revisit only alongside a deliberate tone-class redesign.

items.title: (staging) Design review: distribute Definitions & Caveats into the relevant panels

Delivered a per-item relocation mapping (all 11 glossary + 5 warning items) and a recommendation in docs/backlog-reviews-2026-06-29.md. RECOMMENDATION: HYBRID, staging-first — distribute per-metric definitions to their owning home (AdvancedStats gets a mirror 'Assumptions' <details> for the Payoff/Sortino/Concentration/Streak/Weekday rows; the four F14 stat-card modals net/win/wl/dd absorb their headline definitions incl. 'Max Drawdown is REALIZED only' into the dd modal; CostPanel absorbs the cost/commission/tax notes; EquityCurve/CalendarMonth/scope-toggle get their one-liners), BUT keep the cross-cutting PARSING caveats in one discoverable place (Trade=one closed position; US-dates/Eastern-time assumed) — trim the standalone panel to those or move them to a Help affordance. Axis: context wins for per-number caveats, discoverability wins for the foundational parsing caveats that gate every grouping. Implementation spun off as A97 (staging-first until promoted).

DO NOT adopt @vitejs/plugin-legacy (pulls polyfills/deps against A28 for a modern-browser audience). Asset handling + dep pre-bundling already work with no action. Discussion item — no code change beyond the spun-off A96.

items.title: Review the CI configuration
items.doneNote: Reviewed .github/workflows/{ci.yml,version-bump.yml} against the current Vite+Svelte+native-TS build (written review in docs/backlog-reviews-2026-06-29.md). Pipeline is correct and fails closed. APPLIED low-risk fixes: (1) relabeled the typecheck step (was 'JSDoc via tsc --checkJs' — now tsc core+functions + svelte-check app+site, post-A61/A79); (2) added the missing 'demostore' suite to the unit-test step label (test:unit runs 6 suites); (3) single-sourced the Node pin via node-version-file:.node-version in BOTH workflows (was hardcoded 22); (4) aligned version-bump.yml actions to @v5 (matched ci.yml); (5) added permissions:contents:read to ci.yml (least-privilege; version-bump keeps contents:write to push). VERIFIED correct: full gate ordering; version-bump self-retrigger double-guard ([skip ci] + chore(release) if-guard) + serialize concurrency + push-retry loop; version-bump needs no npm ci (bump-version.mjs uses only node builtins); no secrets consumed (Cloudflare Pages deploys via its own integration). Deferred recommendations (spun off as A96): the CI double-build (build step + e2e webServer both run `npm run build`), Playwright browser caching, and a CI concurrency group. NB the 'use preinstalled Chromium' note in the prompt was about the agent env, not GitHub runners (which DO need the install).

items.title: Discuss: do all the root-directory files need to live at the root?
items.doneNote: Surveyed every repo-root file for keep-vs-move (written inventory in docs/backlog-reviews-2026-06-29.md). VERDICT: keep the root as-is. Almost everything is pinned to the root by a tool's config-discovery (vite/eslint/prettier/svelte/playwright/tsconfig), by npm (package.json+lockfile), by git (.gitignore), or by the host (.node-version is read by both Cloudflare Pages and actions/setup-node) — none can move without an everywhere `--config` rewrite. The ONLY genuinely movable file is vite-ssg.mjs (a plain relative import from vite.config.mjs, nothing discovers it) → could go to scripts/; the three tsconfig*.json are three real TS projects (strict core via tsc, Workers functions via tsc, Svelte+site via svelte-check) and the split is load-bearing, not clutter. Incidental: tsconfig.json's allowJs/checkJs + its comment are now dead post-A69 (no hand-written .js under src/). Optional tidy spun off as A95. No code change — discussion item.

items.title: (staging) New module: Trade Blotter (full-width scrollable trades table)
items.doneNote: Shipped (staging-only). New src/app/components/TradeBlotter.svelte: a full-width, scrollable, read-only trades table mounted as a dashboard module directly BELOW the Trading Calendar (App DEFAULT_ORDER gains 'blotter' on staging only — absent from prod/demo by construction, since sanitizeOrder drops unknown keys and the layout LS namespace is separate). It respects drag-reorder/collapse/hide + workspace templates like every panel, and tracks the active filter set/scope (App passes the already-filtered trades). Columns: Date/time · Symbol (root) · Contract (raw exported symbol) · Side · Qty · Commission · Note · PnL. Every column is read-only EXCEPT Note, which is inline-editable and persists through the shared per-trade annotation path (Store.saveTradeMeta / trademeta), preserving the trade's existing tags/shots; demo-guarded (A87) though it never mounts on demo. Commission reuses the core rateFor() round-turn model (rate×2×qty) — the same rates costModel() uses — rather than recomputing fees. PROMPT WAS STALE → IMPLEMENTED AGAINST CURRENT ARCH: the original referenced the pre-reorg vanilla layer (partials/app-dash.html, <!--IF mode=staging-->, build-includes, DEFAULT_DASH_ORDER), all retired by A30/A33/A69; built on the Svelte SPA instead. Also DROPPED the 'Price' and 'Instrument name' columns the prompt listed: the normalized Trade shape carries neither (price exists only on pre-pairing Fills, discarded after round-trip matching; there is no instrument-name map). Used root for Symbol and the raw exported symbol as 'Contract' (the closest available identifier). e2e: two new specs — staging lists trades, shows the 8 columns below the calendar, and an inline note persists across reload; demo has no blotter. Promote per CH16.

(3) CostPanel.svelte absorbs the two cost notes not already present — the take-home/Net-PnL definition and 'commissions modeled, not exported' — into its existing Assumptions (reconciled: the round-turn/per-symbol/subscriptions/tax/break-even lines already there were NOT duplicated). items.title: Unit-test the untested core deliverables (curveseries + report + costModel qty)
items.doneNote: Added scripts/test-curveandreport.mjs (wired into npm run test:unit) — 28 assertions over the two end-user deliverables that previously had only e2e RENDER coverage, not calc coverage. dailySeries(): one subscription accrual per DISTINCT calendar month, cumulative gross/net/take, take = net − tax(net) only on positive net, single-month + negative-net edge cases, and reconciliation of the final point with costModel.netPreTax/afterTax and compute().net. buildReport(): headline tiles, cost-breakdown rows, and per-symbol commission rows all reconcile with the same compute()/costModel() inputs. Also covers costModel() qty>1 (round-turn = rate×2×qty, contracts = Σqty, linear scaling, Σ bySym = totalComm) and the isoWeek() 52/53 boundary. ENABLER: guarded core.ts's top-level PAGE_MODE document access with `typeof document` so the pure-logic core is importable under Node (it was throwing 'document is not defined' — ironic for the A29 'node-tested' core); the test stubs global.fetch to serve static/data/*.json from disk so loadRefData() populates the real rate tables off-DOM.

items.title: Deploy-contract + version-classification guard (CI)
items.doneNote: Added scripts/check-deploy-contract.mjs (npm run check-deploy; wired into CI right after the build). Two guards: (1) DEPLOY CONTRACT — every static/_redirects target, sitemap.xml <loc>, page <link rel=canonical>, and the robots.txt Sitemap: line must resolve to a real file in dist/, catching a renamed/moved served file that left a dangling reference; (2) VERSION CLASSIFICATION — every git-tracked file under src/ and static/data/ must be recognized by bump-version.mjs's prod/staging/non-shipping rules, failing on any unclassified path so a new directory can't silently bump nothing (CH12). Exported STAGING_ONLY + NON_SHIPPING_DATA from bump-version.mjs for reuse, and switched its versions.json path build to path.join (the audit's 'P1' was actually already correct via the URL trailing slash — applied as a tidy). 14 checks pass on the current tree.

Both the resolve and reject paths bail when superseded.

items.doneNote: Readability sweep. StatCardModal.svelte: extracted the inline anonymous snippet/return object types to named aliases (SplitSeg / SymPfRow / RenderedBar). core.ts: documented isoWeek()'s ISO-8601 Thursday-shift and why Math.round (not floor) is correct at the 52/53 boundary. The remaining audit sub-points were already conformant and left as-is: a scan found NO type-restating JSDoc on component Props (the convention is already followed — existing comments are behavioral prose), and entitlements.ts (INTENTIONALLY UNLOADED + A4/A16/CH35 timeline) and DemoStore.importAll() (no-op rationale) already carry their intent comments.

items.title: Flag unknown futures point-values instead of silently pricing them at $1/point
items.doneNote: Unknown futures point-values no longer silently price at $1/point. pairFills() flags any price-derived trade whose root is absent from the POINT table (pvEstimated on the Trade; estimatedRoots on the ParseResult). Both import paths WARN without dropping data: the landing flow shows a dismissible warn-banner in App.svelte; ManageData appends a ⚠ note to its import message. Fixture in test-adapters.mjs (SR3 fills → estimatedRoots=[SR3], pnl = price diff × $1; a known root stays unflagged).

items.title: Widen the trade dedupe key so distinct same-second trades aren't collapsed
items.doneNote: Widened the dedupe key (store.ts tradeId) with a within-file ordinal: parse() tags the 2nd+ occurrence of an identical time|symbol|side|pnl with t.dup, and tradeId appends |<dup> ONLY when dup>0 — so unique trades keep byte-identical ids (no re-dedupe churn for existing local data) while genuinely-distinct same-second/symbol/side/pnl trades (e.g. two identical TradingView scalps) no longer collapse. A re-upload of the same file reproduces the ordinals and still dedupes. Fixtures: twins both survive + get distinct ids; unique-id backward-compat asserted.

items.title: Multi-lot realized PnL is apportioned by qty, not per-lot price spread
items.doneNote: pairFills() now apportions a broker-provided realized across the closed FIFO lots by each lot's PRICE-SPREAD share (parts sum exactly to realized, preserving per-lot sign/magnitude) instead of a flat qty proration; falls back to qty proration when the signed spreads ≈ 0. Matcher restructured into a plan-then-apportion pass. Existing flip/IBKR realized tests unchanged; new fixture: a SELL 2 closing longs@100/110 with realized 30 splits 20/10 (not 15/15) and sums to 30.

items.title: Mirror the costModel B13 negative-clamp in the equity-curve fixedMo
items.doneNote: EquityCurve.build() now clamps platform/feedCost to ≥0 (Math.max(0,…)) to mirror costModel's B13 guard, so a negative cost input can't make the curve's net/take endpoint diverge from the cost panel.

items.title: Decide: subscription cost over active months vs elapsed span
items.doneNote: DECISION + CHANGE: switched fixed subscriptions to ELAPSED-SPAN accrual — fixedMo × every calendar month from the first to the last trade inclusive (gap months billed), since platform/data fees bill whether or not you trade (active-months-only under-counted a sporadic trader). Implemented in lockstep: core.ts spanMonths() drives costModel.fixedPeriod (and the returned months); curveseries.dailySeries() accrues per elapsed month so the curve endpoint still reconciles with costModel. Copy updated (CostPanel caveat, Definitions, report footer). New gap-month test locks it in (Jan+Mar → 3 months billed; curve reconciles). NOTE: the clarifying question to the user failed to send, so this took the audit-recommended option; reversible by restoring m.months in costModel + the seenMonths accrual in dailySeries.

items.title: Add node fixtures for the A66 calc edge cases
items.doneNote: Added node fixtures for the calc-audit paths: unknown point-value (A113), same-second dedupe collision + backward-compat id (A114), multi-lot realized apportionment (A115), and a DST-boundary holdMs (A120) in test-adapters.mjs; plus an elapsed-span gap-month reconciliation test (A117) in test-curveandreport.mjs. costModel qty>1 was already covered (A98). All suites green (test-adapters 83, test-curveandreport 31).

items.title: Surface the cost-model assumptions (gross PnL, no-qty close events) to users
items.doneNote: Surfaced the cost-model assumptions to users: a new always-visible CostPanel caveat states costs assume gross P&L (double-count risk if the platform already nets fees) and that a close-event export with no per-trade quantity is billed as a single contract; the report footer (report.ts) carries the same note. Subscription/Definitions copy updated alongside A117.

items.title: Minor numeric edge cases: DST holdMs, normTime tz fallback, cent rounding
items.doneNote: tms() now parses timestamps as UTC (trailing Z) so a holdMs DELTA is timezone-independent and stable across a DST boundary (a fixture asserts a 2h wall-clock hold on US spring-forward day). The other two sub-items were DECLINED with rationale: (b) normTime()'s exotic-format new Date() fallback stays as-is — recognized ISO/slash formats are already tz-safe, the fallback is rare and can't be made deterministic without knowing the source format; (c) compute().net cent-rounding left to display formatting to avoid breaking the gross===net curve-reconciliation invariant (drift is sub-cent).

items.title: (staging) Activity Terminal only shows 'staging app ready' — restore the live event log
items.doneNote: Restored the Activity Terminal's live status log. Fix: App emits the boot/status events ('refdata:loaded' + 'data:loaded') from a post-load $effect (runs after the terminal has mounted) rather than inside boot(); added a 'refdata:loaded' → 'reference data loaded' formatter. The terminal now shows the boot sequence plus ongoing events. Applied on all surfaces (pure bug fix). e2e asserts the multi-line boot log.

items.doneNote: Donate button tweaks: dropped the heart emoji (label is now just 'Donate') and openDonate() opens the Stripe page in a NEW TAB — window.open(DONATE_URL, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer') — instead of a popup window. e2e updated to assert the _blank target + noopener and the heart-free label. items.title: Discussion: self-contained dashboard modules for maintainability/scaling
items.doneNote:
Implemented a dashboard MODULE REGISTRY (src/app/lib/modules.ts). Each module now declares itself in one place — key, label, F26 grid membership, surface gate, component, and a pure props selector from a shared DashCtx — and App.svelte ITERATES it, replacing the four hand-synced structures (DEFAULT_ORDER + MODULE_LABELS + GRID_KEYS) and the per-module {#if key===…} render switch (the pre-A108 wiring where adding a module touched 5 disjoint spots). DEFAULT_ORDER/MODULE_LABELS/GRID_KEYS are now DERIVED from the registry; rendering dispatches dynamically via (the calendar's `extra` day-trades/journal child snippet is the one special-case kept in App). App exposes `ctx` via getters so selectors stay fine-grained reactive (reading ctx.metricsAll tracks only that $derived, like the old explicit metrics={…}). A mod

() factory type-checks each selector against its component's REAL props — the only `any` is the erased heterogeneous component field. Adding a module = one registry entry + the component. Behavior-preserving: registry-derived order/gating verified IDENTICAL (staging: cal/cost/adv grid + blotter/term, defs footer; demo: full stack with defs panel), npm test + 45 e2e green, bundle within budget. Deliberately did NOT bundle the A80 .svelte.ts shared-state extraction (kept the blast radius small; they compose).

items.title: Discussion & planning: realtime resizable + snapping modules
items.doneNote: Discussion/planning delivered (analysis in-session). Assessed realtime resizable + snapping modules against the CURRENT layout system: Panel collapse + native HTML5 drag-REORDER of a 1-D panelOrder, the F26 parallel grid (auto-fit columns, align-items:stretch), Store.local persistence + workspace templates, the style-src 'self' CSP with the styleProps CSSOM action for dynamic styles, and NO drag/resize library (A28). Two model families weighed: (A) a TILING dashboard grid — each module owns {x,y,w,h} on a column grid, resize+snap to tracks, neighbors reflow; bounded complexity, maps to CSS Grid spans (CSP-clean via styleProps), compact persistence, responsive, builds on existing primitives — vs (B) FREE-FLOATING dock/magnet windows (literal Quantower) — a windowing toolkit (dock-tree serialization, z-index/overlap), heavy, desktop-only, a11y/mobile-hostile. RECOMMENDATION: do NOT build free-floating docking (wrong cost/benefit for a journal); pursue a tiling grid, PHASED (width-resize within the F26 grid → height → snap+reflow → maybe drag-between-cells), prefer a CUSTOM minimal build (A28/CSP-clean by construction) but evaluate gridstack.js first. Mobile degrades to today's single-column stack; persistence extends the Store.local seam ({key→{x,y,w,h}} replacing the 1-D order, with migration from tj_order); perf via rAF-throttled resize that updates grid spans only (compute() is data-derived, not size-derived, so metrics don't recompute on resize). USER DECISIONS captured: target the tiling grid; evaluate gridstack first. Spun off R20 (gridstack-vs-custom eval, CSP/A28) and F29 (phase-1 resizable grid POC, gated on R20).

items.title: Discussion: styling/stylesheet strategy vs modern conventions
items.doneNote: Styling-strategy assessment delivered (in-session). Verdict: KEEP the current approach — it already matches modern convention AND is ahead of the curve on security; no architecture change. Per dimension: (1) design tokens as CSS custom properties (src/styles/tokens.css, ~20 vars linked by every surface) — the modern idiom (Tailwind v4 CSS-first tokens / shadcn); a token PIPELINE (Style Dictionary) or Sass vars would add a dep (A28) for ~20 tokens and lose runtime theme-ability — not worth it. (2) scoped per-component <style> (Svelte SFC, Vite-extracted; 27 blocks) — idiomatic component-CSS; utility-first (Tailwind) = large dep + paradigm shift against A28, and the scoped model is clean/colocated. (3) no inline style + the styleProps CSSOM action under style-src 'self' (S18/A55; 7 sites in EquityCurve/StatCardModal) — STRICTER CSP than most apps (which ship 'unsafe-inline'); styleProps is a clean contained workaround; keep (nonce/hash inline styles would be more complex). (4) dead/duplicated CSS — svelte-check catches per-component unused selectors (0 today); cross-component duplication is minor but real. Every alternative was weighed against A28 (each is an unneeded dependency) and the CSP (the current model is built FOR strict style-src; utility/CSS-in-JS would fight it). FINDINGS spun into CH36: the .caveats <details> block is near-identical in AdvancedStats + CostPanel; the 'secondary control' button idiom (panel2 bg / --line border / radius / --hover-line hover) is hand-repeated across ~6 components (border:1px solid var(--line) appears 77x); and tokens.css carries a STALE header comment (references site.css/app.css/'bespoke index.html', all retired by A69/A30). — SUPERSEDED (2026-06-30) by A128: full Tailwind v4 + shadcn-svelte + bits-ui + tailwind-variants adoption was approved PRE-LAUNCH, reversing this recommendation, because the product is growing past a simple tool (decision owner's call).

items.title: CSS de-duplication cleanups (from the A104 styling audit)
items.doneNote: (1) Extracted a shared Caveats.svelte component — the 'Assumptions & caveats' <details> block (summary + ul + the li/b/em/i styling) lives once now; AdvancedStats.svelte + CostPanel.svelte render <Caveats>{li bullets}</Caveats> and dropped their duplicate ~25-line .caveats CSS. The bullets pass as the children snippet (the caller's scope), so their li/b/em/i styling uses :global scoped under the Caveats-owned .caveats/ul — CSP-clean (no inline styles), verified identical rendering (li=--dim 12px, b=--txt, em/i italic). (2) Button-idiom DECISION: KEEP the scoped repetition — do NOT introduce a Button.svelte primitive now. Rationale: the ~6-8 'secondary control' buttons vary (size/padding; donate/accent vs panel2-secondary tones), so a shared component needs a variant API that outweighs the small per-site duplication; refactoring 8 call sites across 5 components is disproportionate churn/risk for a P3 cleanup; and A104 favors colocated scoped CSS over utility/global classes (a global .btn utility would contradict it). A Button primitive earns its weight better as the UI grows (e.g. alongside the F29 resizable-grid work) — left for then. (3) Fixed the stale tokens.css header comment (referenced site.css / app.css / the 'bespoke homepage index.html', all retired by A69/A30) to describe the current reality. npm test + 45 e2e green; bundle 175.8/200 KiB.
