items.title: Discussion: evaluate adding bits-ui / tailwind / svelte-spa-router / tailwind-variants / felte
items.doneNote: Per-library evaluation delivered (in-session). Framing: the app ships ZERO runtime deps today (package.json deps:{} — Svelte/Vite are build-time), modal.ts is a ~50-line e2e-covered a11y action (A42), A104 already chose to KEEP tokens + scoped CSS (not utility-first), and the architecture is multi-page Vite (ADR-001) with no client router (the /app/ surface is a single dashboard view). RECOMMENDATION: adopt NONE now. (1) bits-ui (headless Svelte a11y primitives) — DEFER, the strongest candidate: headless so CSP-clean + compatible with our scoped CSS, could replace the hand-rolled menu/dialog/popover a11y in ~7 components, but it'd be the first bundled runtime dep with non-trivial size vs the ~22 KiB A96-budget headroom, and our hand-rolled code is small + working — revisit with a focused POC when complex interactive UI (F29 resizable grid / richer menus) makes hand-rolling a11y costly. (2) tailwind — DECLINE: CSP-compatible actually (it emits a static linked stylesheet of utility classes, not inline style, and is build-time not runtime), BUT it contradicts A104's just-made keep-scoped-CSS decision and means rewriting 27 scoped <style> blocks — large churn, marginal benefit (tokens already give the design-system consistency). (3) svelte-spa-router — DECLINE: architectural mismatch; the app is multi-page (separate mount points + prerendered marketing HTML + _redirects), the /app/ surface has no routes, so a client router has nothing to route and fights the SSG/SEO model. (4) tailwind-variants — DECLINE: a runtime variant-API for Tailwind class strings, moot without Tailwind; the variant need (CH36 button primitive) is better served by Svelte props + scoped CSS, no dep. (5) felte — DECLINE: our forms (cost-setup selects, FilterBar, manage-data edit, journal) are simple runes-bound inputs with no validation-heavy/multi-step forms and NO server submission (local-compute), so felte's schema-validation/async-submit value doesn't apply; revisit only for a future complex validated form (e.g. accounts/billing behind the Stripe scaffold). Unifying thread: zero-runtime-dep posture + A28 earn-its-weight — none clears the bar vs the current small, working, CSP-clean, scoped-CSS implementation. No spun-off adoption items (all decline/defer); the bits-ui revisit condition (F29 / complex UI) is recorded here for when it's actionable. — SUPERSEDED (2026-06-30) by A128: full Tailwind v4 + shadcn-svelte + bits-ui + tailwind-variants adoption was approved PRE-LAUNCH, reversing this recommendation, because the product is growing past a simple tool (decision owner's call).

items.title: Adopt Tailwind v4 + shadcn-svelte + bits-ui + tailwind-variants (full styling migration)
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-06-30) — full Tailwind v4 + shadcn-svelte + bits-ui + tailwind-variants adoption shipped across ~13 green commits. (1) INFRA: Tailwind v4 via @tailwindcss/vite on ALL surfaces (app/demo/staging + the 6 SSG marketing pages + admin); tokens.css -> @theme is the SINGLE source of token values (bg-accent/text-take/border-line/font-mono..., no JS-config duplication); cn() (clsx+tailwind-merge) + components.json + the $ui alias (mirrored in vite-ssg.mjs for the SSG prerender); deps pinned + lockfiled + npm audit clean (A28); preflight intentionally omitted so utilities are additive. (2) PRIMITIVES (src/ui/, alias $ui): Button (tailwind-variants tv() -- realises the deferred CH36 primitive), Dialog, DropdownMenu, Popover, Select -- shadcn-svelte composition over bits-ui, rendered in place (no Portal) so they stay under <main id=sv-app>. ALL hand-rolled UI replaced: the 3 modals (StatCardModal/ExportReport/ManageData) -> Dialog (modal.ts deleted); the Panel module menu + App Add-module menu -> DropdownMenu; the session-status pill -> Popover; EVERY native <select> in the repo -> $ui Select (app surfaces + the 2 SSG admin backlog filters -- proving the primitives prerender + hydrate through the A69 SSG path). e2e selectors updated as markup changed. (3) STYLING: the ~4,575 lines of scoped component CSS across all 28 components converted to Tailwind utilities (parallelised across worker agents, verified centrally). The deliberate HYBRID remainder stays scoped where utilities are worse: the SVG charts (EquityCurve, StatCardModal curve), the CalendarMonth grid/cells, @keyframes + the CSS-only mobile menu + the {@html} SVG :global rules (Home/Nav), the pseudo-element timeline/step-counter (Changelog/Howto), the styleProps-driven progress bar (Admin), Caveats' :global slotted-bullet rules, and SiteShell's base typography. SiteShell + Home base element rules were moved into @layer base so a utility on an element overrides the base per-element (fixed accent-on-accent invisible CTAs). (4) CSP unchanged (style-src 'self' -- utilities are a linked stylesheet, Floating-UI positions via the CSSOM, zero inline style attribute in dist); the A96 size budget was raised 200->400 KiB for the bits-ui + Floating-UI JS (documented in check-bundle-size.mjs; final ~385 KiB). (5) DOCS: docs/adr-002-tailwind-shadcn.md added + the CLAUDE.md styling/frontend conventions rewritten; A104 + R22 superseded and A55's CSP note clarified (Tailwind classes are NOT inline styles). Every commit kept npm test + e2e (47) + build/SSG/manifest-drift + size-budget green; verified desktop + mobile across app/demo/staging + the marketing SSG + admin. (6) BUTTON ADOPTION: the repeated secondary-control / primary / outline button idiom is centralised onto the $ui Button primitive (tailwind-variants) across ManageData / FilterBar / CalendarMonth / JournalEditor / Landing / ExportReport — the distinct red-danger buttons, icon-only chrome, stateful toggles, and chips are intentionally left as bespoke. The primitive defaults type="button" and a no-underline base so buttonVariants() also styles anchors (e.g. ExportReport's "Email a copy" <a href>). — COMPLETED (2026-06-30, PR #80): the phased hybrid was finished as a full canonical re-platform — the in-house $ui primitives replaced by canonical shadcn-svelte components at src/lib/components/ui (bits-ui v2, via the CLI, portal-to-body); the shadcn SEMANTIC token system adopted wholesale in src/styles/tailwind.css (tokens.css DELETED; accent→primary, domain hues on chart-1..5); structure moved to $lib (core→src/lib/core, cn→src/lib/utils); the old pillars (local-compute moat / dependency-minimalism / tokens.css-single-source) retired. Docs: ADR-002 rewritten + CLAUDE.md/architecture.md updated. size-budget 480 KiB. All gates green; pixel-identical.

items.title: (staging) Review: purpose of the 'Tagged trades' Overview stat + what tagging means
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-01) — Written decision delivered: docs/tagging-review-2026-07-01.md. Both premises had drifted: the 'Tagged trades' Overview stat died with the CH16 cutover (nothing to re-label) and the dashboard Tag filter was restored by A159. Decisions: tagging = the user's own two-scope review vocabulary (per-trade tags label the analysis unit — setups/mistakes/strategies — and exist to be filtered/aggregated; per-day journal tags label context and exist to be seen while reviewing), free-form with the A153 canonical form, no enforced taxonomy; the Overview stat stays retired — its actionable successor is an Analytics 'By tag' breakdown whose untagged bucket IS the coverage stat (A165); day tags stop being write-only (A166); tag inputs autocomplete from the existing vocabulary (A167).

items.title: Analytics 'By tag' breakdown (P&L / win rate / count per tag + untagged bucket)

A footnote documents multi-tag counting. Empty state is a hint pointing at the Blotter/Trade Editor. The bucketing math is core tagBuckets(trades, tagsFor) (pure, node-tested in test-curveandreport.mjs: multi-tag counting, disjoint untagged, win tallies); buildAnalytics takes the trademeta lookup as an accessor from App.svelte so it stays pure, and respects the active filter set (built from metricsActive.trades). Also completed the A157 straggler in analytics.ts (core dowBuckets + DOW_LABEL adopted; local WD/loop deleted). e2e: the A159 staging spec now walks tag → filter → Analytics row + untagged bucket.

Prod/demo are untouched (bar never renders, keys unchanged; demo's in-memory local shim keeps demo non-persistent). e2e: create → independent hide → switch → reload persistence → delete falls back to Main.

items.title: (Discussion) Obsidian-style vault storage vs IndexedDB
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-01) — Written feasibility assessment delivered: docs/vault-storage-assessment.md. Verdict: a live user-folder vault is Chromium-desktop-only (File System Access API; Safari/Firefox/mobile can't), OPFS is origin-private (not a vault), and a Tauri/Capacitor shell would remove all limits later. Recommendation: keep IndexedDB as the working store everywhere and ship the vault progressively — Phase 1 legible multi-file vault export/import on all browsers (trades.csv, journal/*.md, trademeta.json, shots/*.png, meta.json; merges by content-hash id + updated-timestamp LWW through the importAll trust boundary), Phase 2 a linked Chromium-only vault (persisted directory handle, debounced write-behind from IndexedDB) surfaced via A132's toggle as Local / Vault / Remote(stub), Phase 3 shell-native if a shell ever ships. Moat unchanged (still zero egress); VaultStore fits the StoreLike seam 1:1 (Store.local stays sync/localStorage). A132 UNBLOCKED with its 'remote' slot redefined as Local/Vault/Remote.

items.title: (staging) 'Add module +' button on the main dashboard (module picker)

items.title: Vite/CI build hygiene: explicit build.target, size budget, de-dup CI build
items.doneNote: All four shipped, zero new deps (A28). (1) vite.config build.target:'es2022' matching tsconfig — no plugin-legacy. (2) scripts/check-bundle-size.mjs (npm run size-budget): zero-dep script sums the JS the built /app/ shell loads and fails over a 200 KiB ceiling (baseline ~166.6 KiB → ~33 KiB headroom); wired into CI right after the build. (3) De-dup'd via option (b): the Playwright webServer now serves the already-built dist/ in CI (process.env.CI ? serve-only : build+serve) so CI builds once (its own step, which also feeds the size budget) while local `npm run test:e2e` stays one command. (4) Added a concurrency group (cancel-in-progress on non-main refs) and an actions/cache for ~/.cache/ms-playwright keyed on the resolved @playwright/test version (install-deps-only on cache hit). Verified: build, size-budget, full npm test, and all 28 e2e (via the CI serve-only path) green.

items.title: Discussion: re-evaluate staging gating now that all surfaces share one SPA bundle
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-01) — Written recommendation delivered: docs/staging-gating-recommendation.md. Decision: KEEP runtime PAGE_MODE/isStaging gating as the staging model (the A33 one-bundle simplicity is load-bearing; promotion stays CH16 = deleting the gate). Build-time defines/tree-shaking rejected (a per-surface build matrix reintroduces artifact divergence to save a few KiB); a separate root rejected (the known pre-cutover drift cost); admin feature flags kept but for prod kill-switches, not proving grounds. Refinement adopted: staging-only features over ~10 KiB (or pulling a new dep) load behind {#if isStaging}{#await import(...)} — the screen code-split made this cheap; today's inventory (A135 DashTabs, ~2 KiB) is under the threshold. Key clarification recorded: the gate is a UX switch, not a security boundary — the staging SURFACE is what the middleware protects. No items spun off (the threshold rule is codified here + in CH16's prompt).

A 'Send feedback' icon button in the topbar actions area opens a Dialog (new part src/app/parts/FeedbackDialog.svelte): a textarea + an 'Open email draft' anchor that builds mailto:contact@blotterbook.com with the app version + surface in the subject (triage; not PII) and ONLY the typed text as the body; copy states nothing is sent automatically and trades/journal are never included. Works on every surface incl. demo (no persistence, no store writes). e2e (demo) asserts the href targets contact@, contains exactly the typed text, and nothing bulk-attached (length bound).

items.title: CRITICAL: exhaustive audit of all data calculations
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-01) — Exhaustive read-only calc audit delivered: docs/calc-audit-2026-07-01.md. Four parallel auditors (adapters/compute/cost+tax/series+report+view-models), every claim reproduced by EXECUTING the real modules under node; the coordinator independently re-ran both P1s and the top P2s before filing. Verdict: the load-bearing math is sound (pairFills FIFO/apportionment, commission+tax arithmetic, gap-month accrual reconciling with dailySeries to 1e-9, drawdown/Sharpe/Sortino/streaks on normal data) — the failures are at the edges: 2 P1s (semicolon CSVs import pnl=2026 silently; DST spring-forward adds a day to the Reports prior-period window), 11 P2s (date-order poisoning, IBKR midnight times, NaN holdMs/FIFO corruption, concPct float-dust '5.4e17%', inception maxDDpct 0.0%, unsurfaced fallback-rate marker + 16-root fee table, tierOf misclassifying SIL/2YY/10Y/30Y/MWE, gross-vs-net Profit factor between preview and export, negative-qty commission credits, unsorted-input negative months), ~20 P3s. Per the charter NO code was changed — fixes filed as A168 (P1 adapters), A169 (P1 DST), A170 (metric degenerates), A171 (tier/fee integrity), A172 (one basis per label), A173 (qty/order hardening), A174 (view-model polish), A175 (the compute/formatting test suites that pin every audited path).

items.title: P1: adapter input-tolerance hardening (semicolon CSVs import pnl=2026 silently; date/time edge corruption)
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-01) — Adapter input tolerance hardened (calc-audit P1#1 + P2#3-6): parseCSV auto-detects ';' (EU Excel re-saves no longer import pnl=year — plus a TradingView belt-and-braces refusal when Time and PnL resolve to the same cell); detectDateOrder decides from anchored full-cell date matches only (a stray d/m fragment in a note can no longer re-date the file); normTime's ISO branch accepts IBKR's quoted 'YYYY-MM-DD, HH:MM:SS' (fills keep their time); garbage-timestamp fills are SKIPPED and counted (skippedFills) instead of corrupting FIFO order or emitting holdMs NaN; rootSym strips F.US. service prefixes and -CME/.CME-style venue suffixes; parse() rejects impossible calendar dates (2026-31-31); ParseResult carries skippedFills/openLots. 7 new fixtures in test-adapters (90 total).

items.title: P1: DST-safe prior-period bounds in Reports compare
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-01) — priorBounds (src/app/lib/reports.ts) rewritten with calendar arithmetic (setDate — DST-safe) instead of epoch-millisecond subtraction across local midnights: the 23-hour spring-forward day no longer slides the prior window a day early for US-timezone users (calc-audit P1#2). Pinned under TZ=UTC / America/New_York / Australia/Lord_Howe in scripts/test-compute.mjs via child processes (A175).

items.title: P2: metric degenerate-value fixes (concPct float dust, inception maxDDpct, pf/tone conventions)
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-01) — Metric degenerate values fixed to one coherent convention (calc-audit P2#7-8 + P3 batch): concPct gates on the CENT-ROUNDED net (float dust no longer renders ~5.4e17%) and is null without net profit; maxDDpct is null for inception drawdowns (no positive prior peak) — UI shows '—'/'from inception' instead of a wrong 0.0%; maxDDdur counts curve indices (inception off-by-one fixed); pf/wl are NaN ('—') on empty/all-scratch sets and ∞ only with wins (costModel.pf now matches — one PF convention); sortino mirrors pf (∞ with positive mean and no downside); tone(0) is neutral (no green $0.00); usd() clamps sub-cent residue (no red -$0.00); usdWhole signs from the rounded value; num() renders ∞ like ratio(); ddPeakIdx/ddTroughIdx finally consumed (Peak → trough row in the drawdown stat modal); unknown-side trades footnoted on the Analytics long/short card (unknownSide in the VM). All pinned in scripts/test-compute.mjs.

items.title: P2: commission-tier integrity — exchange-fees coverage, explicit micro list, resurface the fallback asterisk
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-01) — Commission-tier integrity (calc-audit P2#9-10): exchange-fees.json expanded 16→33 roots (CBOT treasuries ZT/ZF/ZN/ZB/TN, grains ZC/ZS/ZW, FX 6J/6B/6A/6S, SIL, micro yields 2YY/5YY/10Y/30Y, MBT/MET) with micro-set membership + a notMicro list; tierOf() checks the explicit micro list FIRST (SIL/2YY/10Y/30Y price micro), then known/notMicro roots as std (MWE no longer prices micro via the M-prefix heuristic, which survives only for unknown roots); the known:false fallback marker is resurfaced as an asterisk + footnote in the dashboard Break-even module, the Reports preview cost table (commNote), and the text/Markdown exports; TRADOVATE corrected to 0.39/1.29 per side (free-plan rates confirmed via tradovate.com pricing — std was wrongly equal to micro). Fee VALUES are snapshot estimates: ZN ($0.77+NFA) and 2YY ($0.32) corroborated against NinjaTrader's published table; grains/FX/crypto set from typical published broker fee tables (direct CME fee-schedule fetch was egress-blocked) — flag as partial-confidence data, refine when a real statement disagrees. tierOf/rateFor/estimatedCommRoots pinned in test-curveandreport.

items.title: P2: one basis per label — reconcile gross vs net-of-costs across preview/export/KPIs
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-01) — One basis per label across the Reports flow (calc-audit P2#11): the preview KPI strip now shows the SAME numbers as the export headline — 'Net P&L (pre-tax)' = costModel.netPreTax and 'Profit factor (net of comm.)' = costModel.pf (export headline relabeled to match); the prior-period comparison runs through costModel too (like-for-like); statDetail('net')'s description corrected (the figure is BEFORE modeled costs); the tax table's 'Net §1256 gain (pre-tax)' renamed 'Net P&L (pre-tax)' and the net-of-subscriptions modeling choice documented in Definitions (understates tax for non-TTS filers). Preview↔export parity fixture asserts the KPI strings appear verbatim in vm.md (test-curveandreport). The dashboard's gross 'Net P&L'/'Profit factor' cards are separately-labeled surfaces with disclosed bases.

items.title: P2: qty + input-order hardening (negative qty = commission credit; spanMonths clamp)
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-01) — Qty + input-order hardening (calc-audit P2#12-13): the Trade Editor clamps qty to a positive integer at BOTH seams (setNum in TradeEditor.svelte and editTradeCore in dashboard.svelte.ts) — negative qty can no longer turn commissions into a credit, qty 0 no longer silently bills as 1 unseen; spanMonths() clamps to ≥1 when both dates are present, so reversed input order can't produce a negative subscription accrual that inflates net. spanMonths units (missing/same-month/cross-year/reversed) pinned in test-curveandreport.

items.title: Debug the Activity Terminal — it never populates
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-02) — Activity terminal fixed. Root cause: boot events (app:ready/refdata:loaded/data:loaded) fire before the Dashboard mounts and the EventTarget bus kept no history, so the log always opened empty. The core bus now records the last 50 emits with timestamps (busLog()); the terminal backfills from the replay buffer on mount (assigning state without reading it — the first attempt called add() inside the $effect and tripped effect_update_depth_exceeded) then subscribes live. New emits: filter:saved, filter:applied, tab:created; labels reworked ('session initiated', '[data] loaded N trades', '[csv] imported N'); A195 reordered boot so session-initiated leads. Ring-buffer unit fixtures (cap/order/live-unsubscribe) + a demo e2e asserting the backfilled lines.

items.title: Discussion: brokers.json <-> exchange-fees.json relationship
items.doneNote: Delivered as docs/rates-data-assessment.md (2026-07-04, Phase 3 of the CSV/data-mgmt plan). Verdict: KEEP the two-file split — broker-controlled vs exchange-controlled cost components on different clocks; no overlap/duplication; units consistent. Research finding that shaped F30: CME exchange fees change annually (documented Feb-2024/Feb-2025 notices, cited), broker commissions are sticky and unarchived. Effective-dating shipped across BOTH files as optional closed periods ({until, values, source}, partial by design) with current values untouched. items.title: TradingView calc audit: cross-export reconciliation (phantom trades from truncated fills exports)
items.doneNote: Owner-directed calculation audit over the five real TradingView exports, every upload combination (docs/calc-audit-tradingview-2026-07-04.md). Found: importing balance history + order history together OVERSTATED net by +$363.75 (553.50 vs the authoritative 189.75) — the order-history export's limited reach-back mispairs its earliest round trips into phantom trades whose P&L differs, so tradeId dedupe can't catch them. Fixed with cross-export reconciliation (reconcileImport in src/lib/core/intake.ts, wired through importCsv/reimportFile, scoped to the same platform family): inside a closed export's window, a derived trade either matches an authoritative record at the SAME P&L (dedupe+enrich) or is a phantom (dropped/evicted) — both import orders converge to the identical dataset (fixture-pinned, 6 new assertions). Import preview states the resolved count before confirm; Howto updated ('import both — reconciliation is automatic'). Post-fix matrix: all 7 cases produce authoritative or self-consistent numbers with curve↔costModel reconciliation everywhere. Known documented trade-off: two same-platform ACCOUNTS mixed across export types would over-reconcile — the preview count is the guardrail; account-aware classifiers if multi-account ever lands.

The import preview names the detected platform + beta status before confirm. Fixtures: generic/shuffled-header CSVs refuse; every real platform fixture still detects.

items.title: Per-file CSV storage / provenance (redesign CSV Library)
items.doneNote: Shipped 2026-07-04 (PR #104, together with A208 as one coordinated Trade-shape + Store migration), per the owner's 2026-07-04 design decisions (store raw CSV text; fileIds array for overlap; 50 MB budget). Store DB v3 adds `files` + `filetext` object stores — a CsvFileRec per import (name/label, platform, counts, coverage, size, importedAt) with the raw CSV text persisted separately; trades carry a `fileIds` provenance array, and a re-imported duplicate MERGES provenance + ENRICHES missing fields instead of no-op'ing (fileIds/commission are deliberately excluded from the tradeId content hash). The CSV Library lists real per-file records with working include/exclude, rename, re-import, download-original, and per-file delete (all through the Store seam via callback props); a soft 50 MB raw-CSV budget warns from 80%. Later extended by A211 (per-file broker override on CsvFileRec).

items.title: Use real per-trade commissions from the CSV to override modeled commissions
items.doneNote: Shipped 2026-07-04 (PR #104, together with F37), per the owner's 2026-07-04 decision: ALWAYS-OVERRIDE, no global toggle. Optional `commission` (round-turn commission+fees, $ positive = cost) added to Trade and Fill in types.ts; adapters that expose real costs populate it (fills exports via pairFills — e.g. IBKR). costModel() prices a commission-bearing trade at its ACTUAL CSV amount verbatim and falls back to the modeled tier rate otherwise, with the mix reflected in the bySym breakdown so totals reconcile. UI surfaces the distinction: a dagger footnote on the dashboard cost module ('N of M trades priced at actual commissions from your CSV'), per-trade actual-vs-modeled display in the blotter, and CSV Library copy noting real-commission trades ignore the broker setting. Node fixtures assert commission-bearing vs bare CSVs cost out correctly.

items.title: Discussion & planning: Trade Replay (simulated market snapshots)
items.doneNote: Delivered 2026-07-05: docs/trade-replay-r11.md. VERDICT: feasible with caveats — phase it, market data LAST. Evidence: 8/10 adapter formats are fills exports carrying per-execution price+time, but pairFills() currently DISCARDS prices (the persisted Trade has no price fields) — so phase 1's prerequisite is a small backward-compatible core change persisting entryPrice/exitPrice. No export carries intra-trade market data. Licensed CME intraday data is a hard blocker for a free client-side app (delayed-data fee ~$304/mo; Databento/Polygon are per-user-key, no redistribution) → recommended path: BYOD bar-file import (zero cost, zero egress); vendor fetch only as consent-gated opt-in (a candle request keyed by symbol+time leaks trading activity — bundled/hosted data rejected outright). Storage: 1m bars ≈ 40-60 KB/symbol-day → IndexedDB-fine with an LRU cap; lightweight-charts (Apache-2.0) fits CSP if lazy-loaded. Phases: (1) own-executions fills replay ~3-5 d; (2) BYOD candles ~1-2 wk; (3) user-keyed vendor fetch ~1 wk, owner-gated. Follow-up filed: F42 (the phase-0 price capture, useful on its own); further phases stay in the doc pending owner direction.

items.title: Discussion: free SAST options for the CI pipeline
items.doneNote: Delivered 2026-07-05: docs/sast-eval-a112.md. VERDICT: adopt Semgrep CE (free LGPL engine; custom rules can mechanically enforce the no-egress invariant) + osv-scanner (the real gap — A28 pinned the lockfile but NOTHING in CI scans dependencies today). SKIP CodeQL: this repo is private, so it requires GitHub Advanced Security (~$30/active-committer/mo, org plans only — the free tier is public-repos-only); skip Socket.dev (paid for private repos, low value with 2 pinned runtime deps) and eslint-plugin-security (TS false-positive burden). Wiring: a separate security.yml workflow (PR + weekly cron) — custom Semgrep rules + osv-scanner FAIL the build, registry rulesets report-only initially; SARIF upload also needs GHAS on private repos so results stay in logs. Follow-up filed: A220.

items.title: Evaluate gridstack.js vs a custom build for the resizable tiling grid (CSP/A28)
items.doneNote: Delivered 2026-07-05: docs/grid-eval-r20.md. VERDICT: CUSTOM — build F29 phase 1 on the existing styleProps/modOrder/Store.local/A186 primitives. The explicit CSP verdict: gridstack v12+ PASSES style-src 'self' (geometry via CSSOM + CSS variables; the dynamic <style> injection was removed in v12 — verified in v12.6.0 source; v10-and-earlier FAIL), no telemetry, MIT — but it loses on size (~83 KiB minified vs ~22 KiB budget headroom), fights the keyed {#each}+animate:flip substrate, and has no built-in keyboard move/resize. Svelte wrapper libs are unmaintained. The doc includes the concrete F29 phase-1 scope ({key,span}[] layout payload v2 + migration, 12-col CSS grid spans via styleProps, rAF pointer resize staged behind the dirty asterisk, keyboard/aria fallback, span-12 mobile degrade) — F29's prompt updated to reference it. Phase 2 (height/2-D) may re-open gridstack or vendor its DOM-free engine under the standing CSP verdict.

items.title: Discussion: performance optimizations
items.doneNote: Delivered 2026-07-05: docs/perf-shortlist-a136.md (measured: 619.4/640 KiB, boot ~451.5 KiB static). TOP 3: (1) $state.raw for allTrades — every recompute runs ~20+ O(n) passes through the deep-reactivity proxy and all mutation paths already replace wholesale; (2) downsample the per-trade SVG paths (the Analytics underwater curve emits one path command per trade — min/max decimation to ~1-2k points is visually lossless); (3) consolidate boot IndexedDB reads (reloadAll awaits 6 reads sequentially; journalDates duplicates getAllJournal). Negative findings: no adoptable dep removal (the select chunk is intentional ADR-002 weight; lucide tree-shakes), Geist Mono is already a 22.6 KiB latin subset, no redundant $effect work. Budget verdict: HOLD 640 KiB; re-ratchet only if bytes are genuinely removed. Follow-up filed: A223.

items.title: Discussion: add contract expiry / delivery month info
items.doneNote: Delivered 2026-07-05: docs/contract-expiry-a137.md. VERDICT: capture expiry — but DERIVE IT ON READ with zero adapter/schema changes: Trade.symbol already persists the raw export symbol (root is the stripped copy), so expiry is recoverable retroactively via a pure core helper (the inverse of rootSym()'s /[FGHJKMNQUVXZ]\d{1,4}$/ strip). Coverage from the real fixtures: 7/10 formats carry full contract codes (MotiveWave/Tradovate/Rithmic/Sierra/TradeStation/Schwab/IBKR) — but BOTH TradingView adapters (the only verified ones) export continuous 'CME_MINI:MES1!' with no expiry, so the UI must degrade to '—'. Parser handles F-Z months, 1/2/4-digit years (single digit resolved nearest-to-trade-date), refuses continuous/spread symbols; digit-ended roots (M2K, SR3, 6E) are safe. Surface in TradeEditor detail first, muted Blotter column second; rollover analytics deferred. Follow-up filed: F40.

items.title: (Discussion) Changelog email signup + notify on changelog update
items.doneNote: Delivered 2026-07-05: docs/changelog-email-a141.md. RECOMMENDATION: build first-party — a Pages Function signup endpoint + D1 subscriber list + Resend transactional sends, triggered by a new paths-filtered GitHub Actions workflow on changelog.json pushes (NOT inside version-bump.yml — the curated changelog lands in a separate commit from the bump). Cost: $0/mo to ~100 subscribers; ~$20/mo (Resend Pro) toward 1k. KV rejected per A17 (per-visitor writes on a public path); MailChannels' free Workers arrangement confirmed ended 2024-08-31; Buttondown ($0→$9/mo) is the buy runner-up. Design covers double opt-in, one-click unsubscribe + List-Unsubscribe headers, hard-delete erasure, no-tracking GDPR-lite posture, Turnstile + D1 cooldown for abuse (defense-in-depth only per S22), and batch sends under the A15 50-subrequest cap. Follow-up filed: F44 (implementation, owner-gated on the provider account).

items.doneNote: Delivered 2026-07-05: docs/ui-libs-eval-a204.md. items.title: Architect the user-account system (passkeys-only, donations, login/Account sidebar item)
items.doneNote: Delivered 2026-07-05 — docs/accounts-architecture.md (agent-drafted, library facts verified against 2026 sources). HEADLINES: passkeys-only via @simplewebauthn/server v13 (MIT, WebCrypto-only, explicitly Workers-compatible) + hand-rolled D1 sessions per Lucia's successor guidance (opaque token, SHA-256 hash, __Host- HttpOnly cookie, Origin check) — REJECTED: better-auth (framework weight, nodejs_compat, open Workers session bug), Clerk (requires style-src 'unsafe-inline' — breaks our CSP), Auth0 (CSP-safe redirect but identities live on Okta). items.title: Perf quick wins: $state.raw collections, boot IndexedDB consolidation, underwater-curve downsampling
items.doneNote: Shipped 2026-07-05 (mega-batch). (1) The four big reassignment-only collections in dashboard.svelte.ts (allTrades/csvFiles/journal/tradeMeta + journalDates) are $state.raw — compute()/costModel()'s ~20 O(n) passes read plain objects, not deep proxies (comment pins the reassignment-only contract). (2) reloadAll() runs its five store reads under Promise.all and derives journalDates from the journal read (the duplicate journalDates() store hit is gone). items.title: ATAS X importer — xlsx statistics export (Journal round trips + Executions fills)

Intake: isXlsxFile/checkXlsxFile (10 MB cap; the text-path binary rejection untouched); both app intake surfaces accept .xlsx and convert lazily; the derived CSV persists as the file's raw text so F37 re-import/provenance work unchanged. (2) Server erase (2026-07-07): a session-authed, owner-only POST /api/sync/delete deletes the workspace's sync_records rows + R2 ciphertext blobs (paged within the A15 subrequest budget) then drops the wrapped-DEK + registry shell; the cloudsync controller's onErased propagates a local purge to it best-effort; never paywalled; verified by scripts/test-sync.mjs. (3) Cross-device auto-drop: DROPPED (owner decision 2026-07-07) after the design correction showed the only sound path is client-driven tombstone-then-wipe (the server cannot mint tombstones under zero-knowledge, and a vanished-workspace-wipes-local handler is unsafe for the local-first moat) — the accepted, documented limitation: a second device keeps its local copy until it reconciles (its next listWorkspaces omits the deleted workspace and its pull 404s, so the stale copy persists locally but never re-uploads). Limitation documented in docs/synced-workspaces.md.

items.title: (audit) Verify no auth / entitlement enforcement is trusted client-side
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07) — read-only audit delivered in docs/audit-a277-server-enforcement.md. Verdict CLEAN: every protected /functions surface re-checks identity/ownership/entitlement/origin server-side; nothing acting as a security boundary is trusted client-side (the F56 LaunchGate, isStaging/isDemo render guards, and the Entitlements tier probe are UX-only and cannot escalate access). All four attack cases confirmed closed: (1) free-tier session hitting cloud-sync → every mutating sync route (push, workspaces POST, wrapped-ik PUT) enforces callerHasCloud→grantsCloud server-side (402); reads are intentionally ungated but leak nothing (own-ciphertext only, 404 on non-owned, no key server-side). (2) unauth → every route resolves the hashed session cookie via sessionFromRequest (constant-time) → 401, fail-closed 503 when DB/bucket unbound. (3) cross-user → ownedWorkspace → uniform 404 (no existence leak); wrapped-ik/workspaces GET keyed by user_id. (4) wrong-origin/CSRF → checkOrigin 403 on all mutating routes, __Host-/HttpOnly/SameSite=Lax cookie, zero CORS headers, webhook raw-body HMAC verified before provisioning. The only follow-up was a non-security doc nit (push.ts JSDoc said MAX_PUSH_RECORDS 15 vs the enforced 12) — fixed inline in the A254/A265 commit.

items.title: (assessment) Evaluate migrating to a Tauri desktop shell — cross-platform expansion
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07) — assessment written to docs/tauri-shell-assessment.md (vault-assessment house style). Recommendation: keep the browser app primary — a Tauri shell is a DISTRIBUTION investment, not architecture, since compute is already 100% local and only identity/billing/sync are server-side (a shell buys durable storage + OS/app-store reach, not new product capability). If ever built, use Tauri (beats Electron on binary size/mobile/attack-surface, beats Capacitor for a desktop-leaning dashboard): the existing Vite dist/ embeds almost for free and the Store seam ports cleanly (IndexedDB works in-webview day one; a VaultStore over Tauri's FS is the additive Phase-3 win from the vault assessment). Two sharp gotchas: WebAuthn/passkeys DON'T work in Tauri's webview (need the non-official tauri-plugin-webauthn bridge + RP-ID/associated-domains work on the F53 account path), and Stripe Checkout/magic-link returns need custom-scheme deep-links; CSP ports intact bar a scoped connect-src widening to the API origin (backend stays a shared Cloudflare Functions API, never bundled). The real cost is a full second release pipeline (Rust toolchain, per-OS CI matrix, code signing + Apple notarization, an updater channel, store review) forfeiting the web app's 'deploy = shipped'. Phased: do the free PWA hardening (service worker + storage.persist()) now, a ~3-5 day desktop spike only when durable-storage/real-vault/app-store demand materializes, ~3-6 focused weeks to a signed desktop app with the native vault; mobile a separate multi-week initiative.

items.title: Sync API: TTL/compaction for webhook_events and tombstones (unbounded D1 growth)
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07) — bounded both unbounded ledgers via SWEEP-ON-WRITE (Cloudflare Pages Functions can't run a Cron Trigger, so the item's swept-on-write alternative was taken). (1) webhook_events: markWebhookEvent() (functions/_lib/accounts.ts) now DELETEs rows older than WEBHOOK_EVENT_TTL_MS (7d — Stripe's replay/retry window) after each insert — one extra statement on a low-frequency path. (2) sync_records tombstones: compactTombstones() (functions/_lib/sync.ts) drops deleted=1 rows older than TOMBSTONE_TTL_MS (90d) plus their R2 blobs, bounded by COMPACT_PAGE (200) and piggybacked on push() best-effort so it never threatens the A15 subrequest budget (a device offline past the TTL simply re-adds on its next push — the same resurrection window the client already tolerates). schema.sql comment updated from the A265 TODO to the as-built sweep. Tests: webhook_events sweep (test-accounts.mjs) + tombstone compaction incl. the push-piggyback (test-sync.mjs); the mock D1/R2 stubs were extended for < / <= conditions and array R2 delete. Full npm test green.

items.title: Client/server MAX_PUSH_RECORDS drift breaks sync above 12 changed records
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07, R1 audit docs/repo-audit-2026-07-07.md). The client push-chunk cap had drifted above the server cap: src/app/lib/cloudsync-core.ts MAX_PUSH_RECORDS was 15 while functions/_lib/sync.ts enforces 12 (A253, to stay under the CF 50-subrequest budget) and 413s any batch >12 with no client re-chunk → the first full push after a 13+-record CSV import errored (status:'error'). Now that sync is live on prod (CH16) this was a live availability bug. Fixed: set the client constant to 12 with a comment tying it to the server cap.

items.title: reconcileImport authority map collapses duplicate time|symbol|side keys
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07, R1). reconcileImport's fills-vs-closed authority pnl map (src/lib/core/intake.ts) keyed by time|symbol|side in a Map that kept only the LAST value, so two same-second identical-side same-symbol closed records (the A114 dup case) meant an incoming fills-derived trade matching the FIRST was dropped as a phantom. Single-record behavior unchanged (a 1-element array is equivalent); adapters suite green. Kept the original lenient matching (no consumption) to avoid new drop behavior.

Added a cloud-adopt flow — listCloudWorkspaces()/adoptCloudWorkspace() + the WorkspaceSwitcher 'Available in your cloud' section + store.adoptWorkspace — so a second device decrypts the server workspace name and adopts the SERVER's id + DEK instead of minting a divergent duplicate.

items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07). pending/error/status became per-workspace maps (pendingByWs/errorByWs/runningByWs); refreshSyncStatus fully re-derives the active workspace (no stale 'syncing'/'error' after a switch), runningByWs clears in finally even on abort (no stranded spinner), and 'pending' clears only when a push actually ran (not on a pull-only run).

items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07). LaunchGate gained an enumeration-safe 'Lost your passkey?' recovery form and App.svelte handles a pre-gate `?recover=<token>` via completeRecovery, so a logged-out prod user can start and finish an emailed passkey-recovery link even with the account gate armed.

items.title: Auth: rate-limit result discarded on email-send endpoints (+ recover-verify unthrottled) → email-bombing
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07). Added the missing rate-limit guards on the email-send endpoints — email-verify-send and recover-send now return 429 past the threshold instead of discarding the rateLimited() result, and recover-verify (previously the only account route with none) got the standard 5-per-5-min guard.

items.title: Auth: no passkey/session revocation — recovery leaves the lost passkey + all sessions valid; add credential-delete + session cap

Backend + client: scoped POST /api/account/passkey-delete (caller-scoped delete with a last-passkey lockout guard) + session revocation on recovery + a 90-day absolute session cap; and the Account screen's passkey list gained a confirm-gated Remove control (rendered only when more than one passkey is enrolled).

items.title: Sync: first-writer-wins DEK registration (concurrent enable overwrites the wrapped DEK, strands ciphertext)
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07). Server workspace register is first-writer-wins (INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, returns the stored wrapped_dek) and enableCloudSync adopts the returned DEK when it differs from the freshly-minted one, so a concurrent enable across two devices can't strand ciphertext under an overwritten key; covered by a test-cloudsync.mjs adopt assertion.

items.title: Accounts: add an account-deletion endpoint + fix schema FK/orphan cleanup (GDPR posture)
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07). Added an authed, Origin-checked, fail-closed POST /api/account/delete — a two-phase resumable pager clears R2 ciphertext + sync_records via deleteWorkspacePage, then deleteUserAccount does children-first D1 deletes; schema.sql added ON DELETE CASCADE FKs to donations.user_id, recovery_tokens.user_id and sync_records.workspace_id; orphan-free behavior tested in test-sync.mjs.

items.title: Sync UX: actionable error messages + lapsed-subscription + paused states (replace raw status codes + dead ends)
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07). Transport errors now map to actionable copy via syncErrorCopy (401/402/403/413/5xx) with the raw code kept in errorDetail/title; distinct needs-sub (renew CTA) and paused (resume) states plus a neutral 'checking…' while the tier probe is in flight all shipped on the switcher + Account cloud-sync card.

items.title: Sync: close the workspace-switch barrier hole (mergeRecords is abort-blind; runSync overwrites inFlight)
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07). mergeRecords now takes shouldAbort and re-checks it after the async decrypt pass and before each write phase, and runSync/runPush chain inFlight = allSettled([inFlight, run]) so cancelActiveSync awaits every overlapping run — a workspace switch can never merge one workspace's records into another's DB; test-cloudsync.mjs asserts an aborted merge writes nothing and leaves the cursor unadvanced.

items.title: Sync crypto: authenticate index metadata (AAD) so a write-capable server cannot forge deletes/rollbacks
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07). encryptRecord/decryptRecord bind AAD = workspaceId|type|blinded_id|updated|deleted as a v2 GCM envelope (recordAad), so a write-capable server can't forge index metadata — a forged deleted/updated/type fails auth and is skipped (A263 poison-pill resilience) — while v1 legacy ciphertext still decrypts; covered by test-cloudsync.mjs.

items.title: Sync: long-offline device tombstone strand (>90d) + erase-on-one-device 404 self-heal
items.doneNote: DONE (2026-07-07). resetIfStaleGap forces a full re-push + re-pull when the persisted last-sync gap exceeds the 90-day tombstone TTL (long-offline convergence), and onServerCopyGone treats a 404 on an enabled workspace as server-copy-gone — auto-disable, drop the cached key + cursors, and surface a serverGone message with a re-enable affordance.

items.title: Test coverage: the A279 sync state machine (pill transitions, pending, pause, direction buttons)

items.title: Fix the changelog-email GitHub Actions job (Cloudflare bot challenge blocks the runner)
items.doneNote: Fixed via the owner-chosen pages.dev bypass and CONFIRMED end-to-end 2026-07-07. Code: .github/workflows/changelog-email.yml targets the canonical <project>.pages.dev origin (Cloudflare's own zone — the custom domain's Bot Fight Mode had 403-challenged the GitHub runner; free plan can't WAF-exempt it), all Pro-tier WAF-skip messaging removed, secrets documented in the workflow header; functions/api/notify-changelog.ts gained PUBLIC_ORIGIN (functions/_lib/types.ts) so the unsubscribe link stays branded blotterbook.com when invoked at pages.dev (falls back to the request origin); the earlier kj77tb hardening (challenge-detect fail-fast, browser UA, ?version= 425 deploy-freshness gate) turned out to be already merged via PR #123 and was kept. Tests: scripts/test-email.mjs covers the branded-link + fallback-origin cases (70/70). Infra (owner, 2026-07-07): CHANGELOG_NOTIFY_URL=https://trading-journal-4tn.pages.dev/ + CHANGELOG_NOTIFY_SECRET repo secrets; CHANGELOG_NOTIFY_SECRET + PUBLIC_ORIGIN=https://blotterbook.com Pages Production env vars. PROOF: workflow run #6 (commit e5741c8, the 0.68.0 changelog entry) — the first-ever green run — waited out the deploy via three 425 retries then got HTTP 200 {ok:true,version:'0.68.0',recipients:1,sent:1,failed:0}. Runs #1-#5 had never sent (no-op/challenge-blocked). Docs: functions/README.md env-var section + the A141 design doc + the cloudflare-functions diagram record the as-built routing.

items.title: Corner drag-resize: right-column modules can never reach Large + interrupted-drag wedge
items.doneNote: Fixed 2026-07-08 (R1 pass-3 audit, docs/repo-audit-2026-07-08.md; found independently by two audit agents). The A271 drag snapped to (clientX − card.left)/trackW, so a RIGHT-column Medium module (ls/wday on Analytics, cost/term on the default Dashboard) maxed out at ~6 tracks with the pointer already at the grid's right edge — Large was unreachable and the handle silently inert; teardown was pointerup-only, so a pointercancel (touch interrupted, pen out of range) leaked the listeners and wedged the preview span; Dashboard's fillClass read the committed size while the span read the preview, popping the 65vh fill only after release. Fix (in the shared controller, src/app/lib/modsize.svelte.ts — A319): the span is now the DRAG DELTA against the span at grab time, and the delta is DIAGONAL (x + y, matching the nwse cursor) since a right-column handle has no room to travel right but down always has room; pointercancel tears down WITHOUT committing; the rAF throttle applies the LATEST event instead of dropping same-frame moves (a fast drag-and-release used to commit a stale size); fillClass keys on previewSize. Covered by a new staging e2e (corner-drags 'Long vs short' — right column — to Large and back, e2e/staging-redesign.spec.mjs) that fails against the old math.

items.title: notify-changelog: a totally-failed broadcast was ledgered + reported green (never retried)
items.doneNote: Fixed 2026-07-08 (R1 pass-3 audit). When Resend was down, sendEmailBatch returned {ok:false,sent:0,failed:N} but functions/api/notify-changelog.ts still recordSend()'d the version and returned HTTP 200 — the workflow printed success, and every retry answered deduped:true, so subscribers silently never received that release. Now a total failure (recipients present, sent===0 && failed>0) SKIPS the ledger and returns 502 so the workflow's bounded retry loop (which also gained 429 as transient) re-sends — rotateUnsubToken re-rotation on retry is idempotent; partial success (sent>0) still ledgers (a re-run would double-send the delivered subset) and the workflow now parses the 2xx body and emits a ::warning with ok/sent/failed counts. The 0-recipient-ledgers path is unchanged. Pinned by scripts/test-email.mjs: total failure → 502 + not ledgered + a retry re-sends; partial → 200 ok:false + ledgered + re-run dedupes (80/80).

items.title: Extract the duplicated module-size controller (Dashboard ↔ Analytics) into one shared home
items.doneNote: Done 2026-07-08 (R1 pass-3 audit). The A271 rework had copy-pasted the entire DOM-side size controller into Analytics (~110 lines/screen: sizesOfProp/sizeOf/spanClass/SIZE_LABEL/the lastModKey echo-guard $effect/emitLayout/setModuleSize/sizeIndex + the token-identical pointer-resize block) plus the role=slider corner-handle markup and the ⋯-menu Size radio group — with Calendar named as the next sized screen, a third copy was imminent, and the A317 bug had to be found twice. Now: src/app/lib/modsize.svelte.ts hosts createSizeController(kit, {initial,order,emit,grid,narrow}) — a rune-module factory over the pure LayoutKit (modlayout.ts stays DOM-free for the node suite) owning the size map, reseed echo-guard, emitCurrent, menu/keyboard setters and the (A317-fixed) drag path — and the markup lives in two shared parts, ModuleSizeMenu.svelte (renders inside the host's DropdownMenu.Content) + ModuleResizeHandle.svelte. Both screens now consume the same controller (Dashboard keeps its screen-owned modOrder; commitModules = set order + ctl.emitCurrent()). Also removed Analytics' dead animate:flip (its each iterates a constant array that never reorders — the comment advertised an animation that could never run) and its now-unused flip/dur imports.

items.title: Single-source the module key+label tables + dedupe stored layout keys (drift gates)
items.doneNote: Done 2026-07-08 (R1 pass-3 audit). Each screen's module key set existed twice — modlayout.ts as migration data, the screen as a keys+labels table — held in sync by comments only (drift failed silently: migrateLayout stripped the unlisted key from every persisted layout), DEFAULT_MODULE_KEYS was defined twice with the Dashboard export orphaned under a stale A148 comment (App.svelte's template save had moved to modlayout.defaultLayout()), and migrateLayout/validLayout passed duplicated keys through to the screens' keyed {#each}. Now modlayout.ts owns the ModDef {key,label} tables (DASHBOARD_MODULES/ANALYTICS_MODULES + labelsOf); the key sets DERIVE from them; both screens import the tables; the Dashboard-side duplicate export + stale comments are gone (dashboardKit is exported for the shared controller); migrateLayout/validLayout dedupe (first occurrence wins); PAIRED_MODULE_KEYS/ANALYTICS_FULL_KEYS un-exported (internal only). test-modlayout.mjs pins the lockstep derivation, label non-emptiness, and all three dedupe paths (32 assertions).

items.title: Rate-limit /api/notify-changelog ahead of the shared-secret compare
items.doneNote: Done 2026-07-08 (R1 pass-3 audit). notify-changelog was the one sensitive endpoint with no rateLimited() call in front of its auth, letting an anonymous caller hammer the constant-time secret compare at unlimited rate (defense-in-depth only — the compare is sound and the per-version ledger caps blast radius). Now onRequestPost opens with the sibling-standard guard (5/60s keyed 'notify-changelog' → 429), explicitly documented as S22 defense-in-depth that auth never depends on (the limiter stays fail-open by design); the workflow treats 429 as transient/retryable. test-email.mjs covers it with a STATUS_KV stub: 6th request 429s even with the correct secret, and the no-KV fail-open path is unchanged.

items.title: Analytics quick-range presets: UTC/local off-by-one + frozen 'now'
items.doneNote: Fixed 2026-07-08 (R1 pass-3 audit). The 30D/90D presets derived their from-date via toISOString() (UTC) while trade dates and YTD are local — an evening-US user's '30D' started one calendar day ahead — and RANGES was a $derived.by with no reactive deps, freezing new Date() at mount. Now the boundary is built from local Y/M/D and computed at CALL time (rangeFor(label) invoked on click/render; rangeActive re-derives whenever the filter set changes), so nothing caches a stale 'now'.

items.title: CI drift gate: cover build-econ-events (+ make it idempotent) and check-mermaid parity in npm run ci
items.doneNote: Done 2026-07-08 (R1 pass-3 audit). The CI drift gate re-ran only build-manifest.mjs, so editing the deterministic, network-free build-econ-events.mjs without regenerating its committed static/data/econ-events.json (or hand-editing the JSON) passed CI. ci.yml now re-runs build-econ-events THEN build-manifest before the clean-tree check — which required making the generator IDEMPOTENT first: it stamped updated with today's date on every run (the audit's own dry-run dirtied the file with a date-only diff), so it now keeps the committed stamp when re-rendering with it reproduces the file byte-for-byte, advancing the date only when the CONTENT changed. Also: npm run ci gained check-mermaid for local/CI parity.

items.title: Sign-up flow rework: unverified-email squatting reclaim + TTL purge (deferred from A310)
items.doneNote: Shipped 2026-07-08. BOTH halves of the rework: (a) the TTL purge is wired LAZILY at the registration collision point — register-options frees a 30-day-expired never-verified holder on the spot (maybeFreeExpiredUnverified, functions/_lib/accounts.ts) and proceeds as if the email were free; purgeUnverifiedUsers remains as a bounded admin sweep, now via explicit deleteUserAccount child cleanup (never a bare cascade) and BOTH paths skip sync-workspace owners (their R2 ciphertext needs the A305 pager). (b) the proven-ownership RECLAIM flow: register-options 409s with reclaimable:true for a pre-TTL never-verified holder (documented S26(1) extension); new /api/account/reclaim-send (enumeration-safe generic 200, emails a single-use TTL-hashed purpose=reclaim token only to a never-verified holder) + /api/account/reclaim-confirm (deletes the squatter shell, pre-creates a FRESH already-verified account — the click is the proof — claims donations, returns registration options bound to it so register-verify enrolls the first passkey + starts the session; refuses workspace owners; abandoned-ceremony accounts stay reachable via normal recovery since their email is verified). Client: account.svelte.ts (reclaimable state, reclaimSend/completeReclaim), the reclaim affordance in Account.svelte + LaunchGate.svelte, ?reclaim= magic-link handling in Account.svelte onMount + App.svelte pre-gate (mirrors A300). scripts/test-accounts.mjs extended (mock gains sync tables): squat→purge (incl. workspace-owner + pre-TTL + verified guards), squat→reclaim round-trip (enumeration parity, single-use, purpose isolation, verified-meanwhile race, workspace refusal) — 204 assertions green. Docs: functions/README.md, cloudflare-functions diagram, CLAUDE.md endpoint line.

items.title: (discuss + build) Admin: paginated user table to view/edit per-user entitlements (+ audit the entitlement model)
items.doneNote: Shipped 2026-07-08 (design agent + owner sign-off on the model, then built per the approved design). ENTITLEMENT AUDIT (recorded): /api/me derived the tier solely from the Stripe-webhook-owned subscriptions row via grantsCloud (active/trialing; past_due 3-day grace; canceled until period end) — no manual-grant path existed. MODEL: a dedicated entitlement_overrides D1 table (PK user_id; tier/expires_at/reason/granted_by/granted_at/revoked_at/revoked_by — revoked rows KEPT as the audit trail; + idx on expires_at and a users.created_at index for pagination). hasCloudEntitlement(db,user) in accounts.ts is the NEW single entitlement choke point (live override OR grantsCloud(subscription)) read by BOTH /api/me and the /api/sync paywall (callerHasCloud) — a comp behaves exactly like a paid tier and never masks a real subscription. Endpoints (functions/api/admin/): GET users (admin-token-required reads per owner decision; cursor pagination created_at DESC, 25/clamp 100, ?q= escaped LIKE; S25-safe fields + effectiveTier — never sync/workspace/stripe-id internals) + POST entitlement (grant upsert clearing revocation / revoke stamp; granted_by/revoked_by from Cf-Access-Authenticated-User-Email with a (token) fallback). Admin.svelte gained the Users & entitlements panel (debounced search, Load more cursor, inline grant form permanent/30d/90d/custom + free-text reason, revoke, visible audit stamp). deleteUserAccount sweeps the override row. Tests: test-accounts.mjs +34 assertions (237 green) — hasCloudEntitlement matrix, me-flip round trip, 401/503/404/400, pagination, S25 substring scan; test-sync/test-cloudsync mocks gained the table (the callerHasCloud rewire reads it). OPS: re-run the idempotent wrangler d1 execute --file=functions/schema.sql (additive DDL). Docs: functions/README.md (endpoints + schema note), cloudflare-functions diagram, CLAUDE.md. NOTE: the users list costs ~2 D1 reads/user (~51 subrequests at page size 25) — fine on Workers Paid, near the free-tier cap; admin-only path, batch later if ever needed.

items.title: (discuss + build) In-house Stripe subscription form (replace the Checkout redirect) + smooth the get-cloud-sync flow
items.doneNote: Shipped 2026-07-08 (design agent on Opus → owner sign-off incl. the /account.html-too decision → built). PRIMARY PATH: POST /api/subscription/create (session-authed, Origin-checked, fail-closed 503/501) creates the subscription with payment_behavior=default_incomplete + save_default_payment_method, price server-resolved (S13), customer reuse-before-create persisted to users.stripe_customer_id, per-user Stripe Idempotency-Keys, resumes an in-flight incomplete subscription, tolerates both latest_invoice.payment_intent and the 2025+ confirmation_secret shapes; NEVER grants the tier — the signature-verified webhook stays the only subscriptions writer and the client polls /api/me (awaitCloudTier). CLIENT: the shared SubscribeForm.svelte (Payment Element, Link disabled, confirmPayment redirect:if_required for in-app 3DS, poll-until-cloud, hosted-Checkout fallback when Stripe.js cannot load or the endpoint is 501) embedded in BOTH the app Account screen (Subscribe/Renew CTAs reveal it inline) and /account.html (owner decision); @stripe/stripe-js pinned + lazily dynamic-imported (loader stub only — off the boot path/budget). CSP (owner-approved): path-scoped /app/* + /account.html blocks add js.stripe.com (script+frame) / hooks.stripe.com (3DS frames) / api.stripe.com (connect), frame-src gains self (export-report iframe); every other page keeps pure-self. INTENT CARRY: the homepage Get-cloud-sync CTA → /account.html?subscribe=1; the account page latches the intent through login/signup and auto-opens the form for a non-cloud session — the fresh-user dead end is gone. /api/checkout kept as the fallback. Tests: scripts/test-subscription.mjs (17 assertions, suite #18 in test:unit); the cloud-sync e2e subscribe test flipped to the inline-form flow. OWNER OPS REMAINING: set STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY (pk_test staging / pk_live prod) in the Pages dashboard — until then the form falls back to hosted Checkout cleanly; verify the invoice→PI client-secret field against the account's pinned Stripe API version with a test-mode 3DS card (4000 0027 6000 3184) before announcing.
